*Starred Question Nos. 81 to 86
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177): (cid:255)(cid:307) सं(cid:180)या 81.
(cid:174)ी निलन सोरेन ।
कौशल िवकास काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म हते ु आवंिटत धनरािश
*81. †(cid:174)ी निलन सोरने :
(cid:174)ी जुगल िकशोर:
(cid:179)या कौशल िवकास और उ(cid:299)मशीलता म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक :
(क) (cid:179)या सरकार ने िवगत तीन वष(cid:334) के दौरान कौशल िवकास काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के िलए झारखंड, ह(cid:229)रयाणा
और ज(cid:204)मू-क(cid:212)मीर के िलए कोई अनदु ान/धनरािश सं(cid:214)वीकृत क(cid:236) है;
(ख) यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी िजलावार (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;
(ग) यिद नह(cid:233), तो इसके (cid:179)या कारण ह(cid:167); और
(घ) िवगत तीन वष(cid:334) के दौरान उ(cid:265) रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)(cid:253)े (cid:332) म(cid:164) उपय(cid:170)(cid:265)ु काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के तहत िकतने
यवु ाओ ंको (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण (cid:255)दान िकया गया और त(cid:194)संबंधी िजलावार (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है?
कौशल िवकास और उ(cid:299)मशीलता म(cid:253)ं ालय के रा(cid:186) य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा िश(cid:177)ा म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी
((cid:174)ी जयंत चौधरी):
(क) से (घ): िववरण सभा पटल पर रख िदया गया है ।
िववरण
(क) से (घ): भारत सरकार के कौशल भारत िमशन (एसआईएम) के अंतग(cid:170)त, कौशल िवकास एवं
उ(cid:299)िमता म(cid:253)ं ालय (एमएसडीई) औ(cid:299)ोिगक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण स(cid:214)ं थान(cid:332) (आईटीआई) के मा(cid:197)यम से देश भर म (cid:164)
समाज के सभी वग(cid:334) को िविभ(cid:198)न योजनाओ ंके अंतग(cid:170)त कौशल िवकास क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) के (cid:211)यापक नेटवक(cid:170) के
 The sign + marked above the name of a Member indicates that the Question was actually asked on the floor of the
House by that Member.
10.02.2025 11
मा(cid:197)यम से कौशल, पनु ः कौशल और कौशल उ(cid:198)नयन (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण (cid:255)दान करता है जैस े िक (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी
कौशल िवकास योजना (पीएमके वीवाई), जन िश(cid:177)ण स(cid:214)ं थान (जेएसएस), रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय (cid:255)िश(cid:177)तु ा संवध(cid:170)न
योजना (एनएपीएस) और िश(cid:208)पकार (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण योजना (सीटीएस) कौशल (cid:255)दान िकया जाता है।
एसआईएम का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य भारत के यवु ाओ ंको भिव(cid:213)य के िलए तैयार करना और उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) उ(cid:299)ोग से संबंिधत
कौशल से लैस करना है।
गत् तीन वष(cid:334) अथा(cid:170)त् 2023-24 के दौरान झारखंड, ह(cid:229)रयाणा, और ज(cid:204) मू-क(cid:212) मीर रा(cid:186) य(cid:332) म (cid:164)
एम एस डी ई क(cid:236) पी एम के वी वाई, जे एस एस, एन ए पी एस और सी टी एस योजनाओ ंके अंतग(cid:170)त
(cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त िकए गए अ(cid:203) यिथ(cid:170)य(cid:332) क(cid:236) सं(cid:180) या का िजला वार (cid:202)यौरा अनुबंध-I म(cid:164) िदया गया है ।
उपरो(cid:179) त योजनाओ ंके अंतग(cid:170)त िजल(cid:332) को सीधे धन जारी नह(cid:233) िकया जाता है । पी एम के वी
वाई और ज े एस एस योजनाओ ं के अंतग(cid:170)त धन काया(cid:170)(cid:198) वयन एजेि(cid:198)सय(cid:332) को िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत मानक(cid:332) के
अनसु ार (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण लागत को पूरा करने के िलए जारी िकया जा रहा है । जे एस एस योजना के अंतग(cid:170)त
गैर सरकारी संगठन(cid:332) को सीधे धन जारी िकया जा रहा है । एन ए पी एस के अंतग(cid:170)त, स(cid:214)ं थान(cid:332) को
विृ (cid:253)का समथ(cid:170)न के मा(cid:197) यम से (cid:255)ो(cid:194) सािहत िकया जा रहा है । आई टी आई के संबधं म(cid:164) दैिनक (cid:255)शासन
के साथ साथ िव(cid:194) तीय िनय(cid:253)ं ण संबंिधत रा(cid:186) य सरकार(cid:332) / संघ (cid:177)े(cid:253) (cid:255)शासन के पास रहता है । गत ् तीन
वष(cid:334) के दौरान अथा(cid:170)त् 2023-2024 तक ऊपर उि(cid:208)लिखत योजनाओ ंके काया(cid:170)(cid:198) वयन हेत ु झारखंड,
ह(cid:229)रयाणा और ज(cid:204) मू-क(cid:212) मीर रा(cid:186) य(cid:332) के िलए जारी िकए गए धन का (cid:202)यौरा अनुबंध-II म(cid:164) िदया गया है ।
अनुबंध-I
झारखंड, ह(cid:229)रयाणा और ज(cid:204)मू-क(cid:212)मीर रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164) एमएसडीई क(cid:236) (cid:214) क(cid:236)म(cid:332) के अंतग(cid:170)त (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त
उ(cid:204)मीदवार(cid:332) क(cid:236) िजला-वार स(cid:180)ं या

SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY: Thank you, Sir, for giving me this
opportunity to speak in the House today, to put a Supplementary Question. I am
proud to say that under the able and dynamic leadership of our hon. Chief
Minister of Telangana, Anumula Revanth Reddy Garu, we are going to
ambitiously set up the Young India Skill University in Hyderabad. This Skill
University is his brain child with an objective of providing job opportunities to the
students and imparting skill development training in accordance with the
requirements of the local industries, which are having demand, and in
collaboration with various companies under PPP model. A total of 17 priority
sectors have been identified. We will provide training to 2,000 people in the first
year of 2025-26. We will train about 20,000 people every year. The State has
10.02.2025 22
allocated a fund of Rs.100 crore on its part and want to create a corpus fund of
about Rs.300 to Rs.500 crore for this purpose.
This is my question to the hon. Minister. Is there any proposal from the
Central Government to adequately fund this Young India Skill University to make
it a grand success and to realize the goal of a developed nation by 2047?
SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY: Sir, a number of States have established skill
universities as per the State laws. Under the National Education Policy-2020, our
approach is to work on convergence, to build on capacity, create an ecosystem
for skilling starting from schools and later on going into the higher education
system. The UGC has also published guidelines with regard to embedded
apprenticeship undergraduate degree. So, while the State of Telangana that the
Member is referring to is taking strides and an initiative to launch a skill university,
there is no Central scheme as such per se for establishment of the skill university,
but under our various schemes, we can provide support to credible institutions.
DR. MALLU RAVI: Thank you very much, Sir, for giving me this opportunity. In
Telangana, there are a number of ITI institutions and polytechnic colleges. This
is my question to the hon. Minister. Is there any scheme which is going to
establish skill development centres in ITIs and polytechnic colleges in
Telangana? We are preparing a DPR to be submitted to the Central Government
in this regard. I also want to know whether any sanctions have been made for
skill development in Telangana as of now.
SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY: Sir, ITI is a part of our vibrant skilling ecosystem.
That is a national footprint. The hon. Member is talking about ITIs in the State of
10.02.2025 23
Telangana. I would like to make this House aware that in the last year’s Budget
also there was a very important announcement that the Central Government is
going to dedicate resources towards upgradation, providing infrastructure
support, revitalizing, bringing in new course and curriculums, and teacher
training programs in ITIs. A total of Rs.60,000 crore is to be spent under this
scheme. I think a number of ITIs in Telangana could be covered under this
scheme. Thank you.

GOLD LOAN NPAs IN BANKS AND NBFCs
*82. †SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA:
SHRIMATI KANIMOZHI KARUNANIDHI
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether gold loan NPA has been increased by 30% in the first quarter of
2024-25 as per the data of RBI;
(b) if so, the details of Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) arising from gold loans
during the last five years, year-wise, bank-wise and Non-Banking Finance
Company (NBFC)-wise;
(c) whether commercial banks have reported 62% rise in gold loan NPA to
Rs. 2445 crore in June, 2024 from Rs. 1513 crore in March, 2024 and if so, the
details thereof, bank-wise;
10.02.2025 24
(d) the percentage of gold loan NPAs in the overall loan portfolio of banks and
Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) during the current financial year;
and
(e) the steps taken by the Government to mitigate the risk of NPAs in the gold
loan sector along with the measures taken to improve credit access for small
borrowers while ensuring proper valuation and risk assessment in gold loan
disbursals?

SHRIMATI KANIMOZHI KARUNANIDHI: Sir, I had asked about the Non-
Banking Financial Companies. … (Interruptions)
DR. SHASHI THAROOR: Sir, the question has not been answered. …
(Interruptions) The question was about the Non-Banking Financial Companies.
… (Interruptions)
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN): Sir, if you permit me, the Non-Banking
Financial Companies and scheduled commercial banks, all of them -- are guided
by similar rules. And as the Minister of State explained, there is a process of
giving enough number of notices to the bank account holder to say that your
servicing is not up to the mark and that in the eventuality of you not coming back
to pay we will be forced to go on an auction.
Now, even for going on an auction, there are very well laid-out procedures.
It cannot be done outside of the district; it cannot be done without the presence
of the person; it cannot be done unless the valuation is done; and it is also not
done at a random rate. There is a benchmark rate that the NBFC has to fix, and
that will not be lower than 80 per cent, I think, over the last three or four months
of gold rate. The gold rate is not determined by the NBFC. It is determined by
the Bombay Bullion Association, I think, who gives out the rate every day.
Therefore, it cannot be lower than that rate. So, these are well laid tight
processes.
I would rather have more information from the hon. MP if these processes
are violated, and if they are violated, indeed it is for us to act on. But the
10.02.2025 30
processes are very much in detailed fashion written down and followed by the
banks. And, the reserve price is something which they have to declare well in
time. So, I think that if there are specific instances where the hon. MP thinks that
these laid-out norms are violated, I am quite willing to take the details and act on
it.
SHRI MANISH TEWARI: Thank you very much. Mr. Speaker, Sir.
Mr. Speaker, Sir, with your permission, may I ask a slightly different
question pertaining to gold? In the year 1971, Richard Nixon, who was then
President of the United States of America, gave up the gold standard. After that,
gold was hardly considered to be a serious financial asset. In 2006, gold
constituted only six per cent of the reserves of the central banks of the world. In
the year 2024, it is up to 11 per cent. China, India, Poland and Turkey are the
most aggressive buyers. Even in India, there has been a surge in the domestic
price of gold.
My question to the hon. Minister is this. Does this shift away from the dollar
to gold indicate a renewed search for an alternative international settlement
mechanism, that is an alternative to the dollar dominated system? This is my
question to the hon. Minister.
SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN: Sir, the hon. Member has himself
mentioned that it is not the core part of the question, but he is still asking and I
would certainly like to give my brief answer on it.
As the hon. Member rightly observed, the demand for gold in India has not
come down. On the contrary, it is going up. I think it is very typical of India,
10.02.2025 31
particularly for households or small businesses – and it is normally said ‘women’
– but general households and women also do invest in gold because they think
it is far more secure and far more liquid. Therefore, as an asset class, we see
the sustained interest in it by the households and small businesses as much as
women of India.
The Reserve Bank is buying gold to make sure that it has adequate
reserves of gold and not just a basket of currencies. In that, one of the currencies,
which has predominantly been held by the Reserve Bank, has been the US dollar
but we also have the reserve currencies in different denominations of other
currencies. However, gold is also kept in the Reserve Bank and gold is also being
bought by the Reserve Bank. But beyond that, as regards an international
currency, or potential currency, there is not much for me to comment at this
stage.

DR. M. K. VISHNU PRASAD: Hon. Speaker Sir, nowadays so many skill
development centres have come up. It is indeed a boon for all the youngsters to
develop their skill and contribute to development of the nation. But some skill
development centres have an access to or nexus with some corporates. The
CSR funds of the corporates are being utilized for skill development centres. At
this point of time, I would like to know whether the corporates have the provision
of allocating CSR funds in developing the skill development centres. There are
two ways of thinking. The CSR fund has to be utilized for the development of
community. By giving skill development, of course the community is developed,
but eventually the company is also getting developed. Is there a provision in the
Union Government whereby the CSR fund can be utilized for skill development?
SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY: Absolutely, Sir. Corporate India is providing job
opportunities for youth. They are considered partners in the entire skilling
schemes. The hon. Member spoke about the community impact. We actually
have a dedicated scheme Jan Shikshan Sansthan which works through
grassroot organizations for community impact. As far as corporate India giving
CSR funds, it is of course welcomed. The NSDC is also CSR-compliant and even
in the newly announced ITI upgrade scheme, there is a proposal to involve the
corporate industries, local industry clusters, MSMEs so that they can coordinate
with the skilling institutions, especially ITIs, and they are also required to
10.02.2025 36
contribute CSR funds upto Rs.10,000 crore in that particular scheme.

SHRI MUKESHKUMAR CHANDRAKAANT DALAL: The Government of India
under the leadership of Shri Narendrabhai Modi has done extremely well in the
field of education, particularly higher education during the past decade.
My question to the hon. Minister is this. Will the hon. Minister of Education
be pleased to state: the details of Budget allocation for the Credit Guarantee
Fund for Education Loan; the data on total number of students benefitted from
this Fund in the last five years along with their geographical and demographic
distribution particularly in Gujarat; the details of results observed on access to
higher education and loan repayment rates, supported by relevant data; and
lastly, the details of mechanisms put in place to monitor and evaluate the
effectiveness of the Credit Guarantee Fund for Education Loan and the key
performance indicators tracked?
(cid:174)ी धम(cid:165)(cid:198)(cid:254) (cid:255)धान : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, भारत सरकार (cid:255)धान मं(cid:253)ी उ(cid:184)चतर िश(cid:177)ा (cid:255)ो(cid:194)साहन योजना म(cid:164)
कई (cid:255)कार क(cid:236) (cid:255)ो(cid:194)साहन योजनाए ं चलाती ह(cid:167), िजसम(cid:164) (cid:248)े िडट गारटं ी फं ड्स (cid:214)क(cid:236)म भी एक है । उसम(cid:164)
अभी तक िपछले साल 31 िदस(cid:204)बर, 2024 तक 3 हजार 19 करोड़ (cid:321)पये का आवंटन िकया गया है
। माननीय सद(cid:214)य ने िपछले 5 साल(cid:332) के लाभाथ(cid:234) छा(cid:253)(cid:332) क(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या पूछी थी, जो 6 लाख 78 हजार ह ै
। (cid:255)देशवार उसक(cid:236) संल(cid:181)न सूची दी गई ह ै । िवशेष (cid:322)प से उ(cid:198)ह(cid:332)ने गजु रात के बारे म(cid:164) पूछा ह ै । गजु रात
म(cid:164) 15 हजार 501 िव(cid:299)ाथ(cid:234) उसके लाभाथ(cid:234) बने ह(cid:167) ।
SHRI MUKESHKUMAR CHANDRAKAANT DALAL: My supplementary
question is this. According to a 2022 report by the Union Education Ministry, only
four per cent of higher education students in India currently benefit from
education loans. The Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Education Loan
provides much-needed financial assistance but the maximum loan eligibility
10.02.2025 60
remains capped at Rs. 7.5 lakh, which is insufficient considering the rising cost
of education both in India and abroad.
For studies abroad, students are required to pay a 15 per cent margin
money for loans above Rs. 4 lakh and up to Rs. 7.5 lakh, further increasing their
financial burden. Given that the tuition fees and living expenses have significantly
increased, will the Government consider revising the education loan cap and
reducing the margin money requirement ensuring that students, especially from
middle-class and economically weaker backgrounds can pursue higher
education without financial constraints?

भरने के िलए हमने ए(cid:200)लीके शंस मांगी ह(cid:167) । उसक(cid:236) ला(cid:214)ट डेट 31 जनवरी थी । मई म(cid:164) उसका इंटर(cid:211)यू
होगा और लोग जॉइन भी करग(cid:164) े । इसी तरह से नस(cid:165)स के पद(cid:332) को भरने के िलए हमने ए(cid:204)स को कहा है।
574 नस(cid:165)स के पद(cid:332) को भरने के िलए हमने ए(cid:204)स के साथ कोलैबरेट िकया है । ए(cid:204)स िसले(cid:179)ट करके
ईएसआई को देगा । ए(cid:181)जाम हो गया है और 1930 पद(cid:332) को भरने के िलए हमने यूपीएससी से कहा है ।

हम ए(cid:204)स के साथ काम कर रहे ह(cid:167) । ए(cid:204)स िसल(cid:179)े ट करेगा और ईएसआई को देगा ।

10.02.2025 68
WRITTEN ANSWERS TO QUESTIONS
(i) Starred Question Nos. 87 to 100
LIST OF WILFUL DEFAULTERS
*87. PROF. SOUGATA RAY:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Reserve Bank of India prepared the list of wilful defaulters,
failing to repay bank loans despite having the means to do so;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether a wilful default happens when the borrower has not utilized the
finance from the lender for the specific purpose for which finance was availed or
has the funds diverted for other purposes or siphoned off the funds or disposed
of or removed the movable fixed assets or immovable property without the
knowledge of the bank;
(d) if so, the details thereof; and
(e) the steps taken by the Government to ensure the recovery of loans from
such defaulters?
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a) to (e): RBI, vide Master Direction on Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large
Defaulters, has advised lenders to submit the list of wilful defaulters to all Credit
Information Companies (CICs) on a monthly basis, and CICs are required to
display the same on their respective websites. The details of the same are
10.02.2025 69
available on and can be accessed at the following URL of CICs, registered with
and regulated by RBI:
(i) suit.cibil.com,
(ii) suit.experian.in,
(iii) equifax.co.in, and
(iv) Crifhighmark.com
It is stated in the aforesaid Master Direction that a wilful default by a
borrower occurs when the borrower defaults in meeting payment/repayment
obligations to the lender and any one or more of the following features are
noticed:
(a) the borrower has the capacity to honour the said obligations;
(b) the borrower has diverted the funds availed under the credit facility
from lender;
(c) the borrower has siphoned off the funds availed under the credit facility
from lender;
(d) the borrower has disposed of immovable or movable assets provided
for the purpose of securing the credit facility without the approval of the
lender; and
(e) the borrower or the promoter has failed in its commitment to the lender
to infuse equity despite having the ability to infuse the equity, although
the lender has provided loans or certain concessions to the borrower
based on this commitment and other covenants and conditions.
10.02.2025 70
Banks initiate recovery actions in NPA accounts, including those pertaining
to willful defaulters, under the various recovery mechanisms available, such as,
filing of suits in civil courts or in Debt Recovery Tribunals, action under the
Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of
security Interest (SARFAESI) Act, filing of cases in the National Company Law
Tribunal under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, through negotiated
settlements/ compromise, and through sale of non-performing assets.
Further, comprehensive measures have been taken to deter wilful default
and to recover NPAs, including those pertaining to wilful defaulters. Such
measures include, inter alia, the following:
(i) Change in credit culture has been effected, with IBC fundamentally
changing the creditor-borrower relationship, taking away control of the
defaulting company from promoters, and debarring wilful defaulters
from the resolution process.
(ii) The SARFAESI Act and The Recovery of Debt and Bankruptcy Act have
been amended to make it more effective.
(iii) Wilful defaulters are not sanctioned any additional facilities by banks or
financial institutions, and their unit is debarred from floating new
ventures for five years. Further, wilful defaulters and companies with
wilful defaulters as promoters/directors have been debarred from
accessing capital markets to raise funds.
10.02.2025 71
(iv) As per RBI’s Master Direction on Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and
Large Defaulters, banks may initiate criminal proceedings against wilful
defaulters in eligible cases.
(v) For effective action against fugitive offenders, including wilful defaulters,
the Fugitive Economic Offenders Act, 2018 has been enacted which
provides for attachment and confiscation of property of fugitive
offenders and has disentitled them from defending any civil claim.
RATIONAL OF DRAFT UGC REGULATIONS, 2025
*88. SHRI T.M. SELVAGANAPATHI:
SHRI DINESH CHANDRA YADAV:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the salient features and objectives of the Draft Regulations, 2025 published
by University Grants Commission (UGC);
(b) whether it is a fact that many provisions in the Draft Regulations, 2025 of the
University Grants Commission may pose serious challenges to the academic
integrity, autonomy and inclusive development of State Universities;
(c) whether it is a fact that these Regulations are clear infringement of the rights
of State Governments and would have far-reaching consequences for the
autonomy of State Universities;
(d) whether it is also true that many State Legislative Assemblies have passed
unanimous resolutions against the draft UGC regulations; and
10.02.2025 72
(e) if so, whether the Government proposes to withdraw or to rectify the
shortcoming of these draft regulations?
THE MINISTER OF EDUCATION (SHRI DHARMENDRA PRADHAN):
(a): The University Grants Commission (UGC) has released the draft UGC
(Minimum Qualifications for Appointment & Promotion of Teachers and
Academic Staff in Universities and Colleges and Measures for the Maintenance
of Standards in Higher Education) Regulations, 2025. Its objective is to infuse
innovation, inclusivity, flexibility and dynamism in every aspect of higher
education, empower teachers and academic staff, strengthen academic
standards and pave the way for achieving educational excellence and to make it
in sync with the ethos of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. It strives for
driving transformations in the education system and propelling the country
towards Viksit Bharat 2047 through quality education and research.
(b) to (e): UGC is empowered to make regulations for the promotion and co-
ordination of University education and for the determination and maintenance of
standards of teaching, examination and research in Universities. UGC notifies
regulations in the exercise of powers conferred under clause (e) of sub-section(I)
of Section 26 of the University Grants Commission Act, 1956 (3 of 1956). The
draft UGC Regulations, 2025 provide more autonomy and inclusive development
of the State Universities. The draft regulations provide more power to universities
in the selection process of teachers. The selection committees of universities will
decide on the quality of research publications, the reputation of publishers, etc.,
based on the input from external experts, rather than the fixed numerical score
10.02.2025 73
known as Academic Performance Index (API). The eligibility criteria for
appointment and promotion have been simplified and broadened in the draft
regulations. It empowers State Governments to decide on the selection process
of teachers in colleges falling under their purview. These draft regulations have
been placed in the public domain for feedback, suggestions and wide
consultations and the last date to receive feedback from stakeholders has been
extended to 28.02.2025. Various suggestions, comments and feedback have
been received from different stakeholders. The feedback so received will be
analysed by an Expert Committee for incorporating suitable suggestions in the
regulations.
WELFARE OF BANKING CORRESPONDENTS
*89. DR. K. SUDHAKAR:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has noticed that large number of banking
correspondents today face the issues of survival due to unrevised commission
structures and outdating incentives that doesn’t adjust with rising inflation levels
and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the steps taken by the Government to ensure that banking correspondents
channel remains most active to ensure last mile banking connectivity for rural
areas of our country along with the steps taken by the Government for the welfare
of banking correspondents;
10.02.2025 74
(c) the total number of banking correspondents currently providing increased
financial inclusion for the state of Karnataka; and
(d) the total number of Fintech companies currently operational in the country
with their total business output and their contribution in today’s credit flow
markets, State-wise?
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a) and (b): As per the extant guidelines of Reserve Bank of India (RBI) dated
01.07.2014, Scheduled Commercial Banks, including Regional Rural Banks and
Local Area Banks, have been permitted to provide financial and banking services
through the use of Business Correspondents (BCs).
Banks formulate a policy for engaging BCs with the approval of their Board
of Directors’ wherein the banks may pay reasonable commission/fee to the BCs,
the rate and quantum of which is reviewed periodically. Further, Indian Banks’
Association (IBA) has, in consultation with Banks and Indian Institute of Banking
and Finance (IIBF), developed suitable training modules in the local language(s)
for providing proper attitudinal orientation and skills to the BC agents.
BCs get commission for each transaction they perform. Over the years,
number of services, which a customer can avail at BC point, has increased and,
as on date, BCs are providing up to 56 services to their customers. These include
transaction services, PMJDY account opening, deposit mobilisation, loan leads,
NPA recovery, Micro insurance, recurring deposit etc. thereby increasing the
opportunities available to BCs for earning higher commissions by rendering
10.02.2025 75
these services to the customers. Further, for BCs operating in hilly areas and
North-Eastern States, NABARD has launched a scheme wherein an incentive of
Rs. 1,000/- per month per BC is given to two top performing BCs per village.
(c): The total number of fixed point inter-operable banking correspondents
working in the state of Karnataka as on 31.01.2025 is 56,607.
(d): FinTechs mostly collaborate with Regulated Entities (REs) like banks and
NBFCs to provide innovative services/solution to them which brings in speed and
efficiencies in various aspects of their working. Generally, these entities work in
collaboration with REs as outsourced agencies and are governed under the
outsourcing guidelines of the Reserve Bank. The data relating to these
companies is not maintained centrally.
REWORKING ON PM INTERNSHIP SCHEME TO BOOST EMPLOYMENT
QUESTION
*90. SHRI SUDHEER GUPTA:
SHRI DHAIRYASHEEL SAMBHAJIRAO MANE:
Will the Minister of CORPORATE AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is reworking on Prime Minister Internship Scheme
aimed at boosting employment in the country and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has launched a pilot phase in some of the States
under the said scheme;
(c) if so, the details thereof and the outcome and learning of the pilot phase,
State-wise and district-wise;
10.02.2025 76
(d) whether the Government has finalised the modalities for operating the said
scheme and if so, the details thereof and the time by which the said scheme is
likely to be rolled out completely; and
(e) the details of total amount earmarked/sanctioned/utilised under the said
scheme since its inception?
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a) to (d): The Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme (PMIS) announced in the
Budget 2024-25, aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore youth in
top 500 companies in five years. Through this Scheme, youth will gain exposure
for 12 months to real-life business environment, across varied professions and
employment opportunities with the intention of enhancing their employability by
bridging the gap between academic learning and industry requirements. As an
initiation to PMIS, Ministry of Corporate Affairs has launched a Pilot Project of
the Scheme on 3rd October, 2024, which is targeted at providing 1.25 lakh
internship opportunities in the Financial Year 2024-25. Guidelines containing
details for Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme - Pilot Project are available at
https://pminternship.mca.gov.in. In Round I of the Pilot Project, Companies
offered more than 1.27 lakh opportunities across different sectors, across all
States/UTs. The second round of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project has
started from 9th January, 2025, and the companies are in the process of posting
new as well as editing unfilled internship opportunities.
10.02.2025 77
The Pilot Project is a pivotal phase that allows for testing concepts,
strategies and systems before a full-scale implementation. Based on the
feedback received and evaluation of outcomes during the implementation of the
Pilot Project, lessons drawn will be factored in while rolling out the first phase of
the PM Internship Scheme, as announced in the Budget 2024-25.
The Guidelines of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project provide for
constitution of a Monitoring and Steering Committee comprising of all
stakeholders including industry representatives to oversee design
implementation, operations and other aspects. Further, a concurrent Monitoring,
Evaluation and Learning (MEL) framework is also provided for to enable tracking
of the outcomes as well as ensure corrective actions during the course of
implementation of the pilot project.
(e): For the Pilot Project of the Scheme, an amount of Rs. 840 crores have been
approved. So far, approximately Rs.48 crore have been spent so far.
IMPACT OF GST ON LEASED PROPERTIES
*91. SHRIMATI SAJDA AHMED:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any plan to roll back imposition of GST on
leased properties as it gives the financial burden on tenants and property owners;
(b) whether the Government has conducted any studies or consultations to
assess the economic impact of GST on leased properties, if so, the details
thereof; and
10.02.2025 78
(c) the steps taken by the Government to survey and maintain accurate data on
leased properties across the country?
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a) and (b): GST rates or exemption is prescribed based on the recommendation
of the GST Council, which is a constitutional body having representatives of both
Centre and States. GST is leviable on renting/leasing of property @ 18% since
the introduction of GST in 2017. GST Council in its 54th meeting had
recommended bringing the levy of GST on renting/leasing of non- residential
(commercial) property under reverse charge basis. Earlier this was taxable under
forward charge basis. Accordingly, an entry at Sl. No. 5AB was inserted in
Notification No. 13/2017-CT(R) dated 28.06.2017 vide Notification No. 09/2024-
CT(R) dated 08.10.2024 . Later on it was represented that this change is causing
additional burden on registered assessees who have opted for Composition
Scheme, as they are not entitled to claim ITC of such GST paid.
Accordingly, the matter was deliberated in detail in 55th GST Council
meeting held on 21.12.2024. The Council recommended that assessees working
under composition levy should be excluded from the scope of Sl. No. 5AB of the
Notification No. 13/2017-CT(R) dated 28.06.2017. Accordingly, Notification No.
07/2025 – CT(R) dated 16.01.2025 was issued to exclude composition taxpayers
from the requirement of paying GST on reverse charge basis on such services
of renting/leasing of immovable property received from unregistered persons.
10.02.2025 79
(c) : The data about the properties leased across the country is not maintained
by the Central Government.
ECO AND HERITAGE TOURISM IN ASSAM
*92. SHRIMATI BIJULI KALITA MEDHI:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) the manner in which the Government is planning to promote Eco tourism
and Heritage tourism in Assam; and
(b) the details of the specific steps being taken to enhance facilities at major
tourist destinations like Kaziranga and Kamakhya Temple?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) and (b): Development and promotion of tourist destinations and products,
including Eco and Heritage Tourism, is undertaken by the respective State
Government/Union Territory (UT) Administration. The Ministry complements the
efforts of States/UTs by developing and promoting various tourism products of
the country, including Eco and Heritage Tourism in Assam through various
schemes and initiatives.
Ministry of Tourism annually organizes International Tourism Mart (ITM) in
the North Eastern region. This event serves as a vital platform, bringing together
tourism businesses and entrepreneurs from the North Eastern States to foster
collaboration and interactions among buyers, sellers, media, government
agencies, and other stakeholders. Ministry of Tourism organized the 12th edition
10.02.2025 80
of the International Tourism Mart (ITM), in Kaziranga from November 26th to 29th,
2024.
In order to position India as a preferred global destination for Eco Tourism,
Ministry of Tourism has formulated National Strategy for Eco –Tourism.
The Ministry of Tourism through its central sector schemes of ‘Swadesh
Darshan (SD)’, ‘Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage Augmentation
Drive (PRASHAD)’ and ‘Assistance to Central Agencies for Tourism
Infrastructure Development’ complements the efforts of tourism infrastructure
development in the country by extending financial assistance to the State
Governments/UT Administrations including the State of Assam.
Under the Swadesh Darshan Scheme two projects were sanctioned in
Assam one under Wildlife Circuit which also includes interventions at Kaziranga
and another under Heritage Circuit. Details of these projects are Annexed.
Ministry of Tourism has revamped the Swadesh Darshan Scheme as
Swadesh Darshan 2.0 (SD 2.0) with the objective to develop sustainable and
responsible tourism destinations, following a destination and tourist-centric
approach. Kokrajhar and Jorhat have been identified for development under it.
The Ministry of Tourism has also issued guidelines for ‘Challenge Based
Destination Development’, a sub-scheme under Swadesh Darshan 2.0 with the
aim for holistic development of destination to enhance tourist experience. Details
of destination identified for development in the State of Assam is Annexed.
Under the PRASHAD Scheme, the Ministry has sanctioned a project for
'Development of Pilgrimage amenities at Kamakhya Temple'
10.02.2025 81
With a view to providing enhanced amenities and facilities for tourists, a
project ‘Joint Development of Kamakhya Railway Station’ was sanctioned by the
Ministry of Railways and Ministry of Tourism on 50:50 cost sharing basis.
The Government of India under the guidelines for Special Assistance to
States/Union Territories for Capital Investment (SASCI) for Development of
Iconic Tourist Centres has approved two projects in Assam.
Details of projects sanctioned for the State of Assam under the aforesaid
schemes of Ministry of Tourism are given in the enclosed Statement.
As informed by the Government of Assam, to enhance the facilities at
Kaziranga, the project “Upgradation of Tourist facilities at Kaziranga” sanctioned
by North Eastern Council in the year 2021-22 has been executed by the
Government of Assam and the project is completed in Nov, 2024. Moreover,
under the Amar Alohi Scheme of Government of Assam, in the FY 2017-18 and
2018-19, sixteen beneficiaries had been selected at Kaziranga for development
and promotion of homestays and the works had been completed accordingly.

और आवास एवं (cid:214)थानीय प(cid:229)रवहन (cid:255)दान िकया जाता है तािक वे अपना जीिवकोपाज(cid:170)न कर सक(cid:164) ।
इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:179) त, सभी सात जेडसीसी (cid:177)े(cid:253)ीय महो(cid:194) सव(cid:332) और रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय सं(cid:214)कृित महो(cid:194)सव (आरएसएम)

PROGRAMMES FOR WOMEN WORKING ON DAILY WAGES
*94. SHRI A. MANI:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether there is any targeted programmes for women working as daily wage
earners and if so, the details thereof; and
(b) the details of steps being taken by the Government to address the issues
such as wage disparity and workplace safety for them?
10.02.2025 88
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF
YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
(a) and (b): Government has taken legislative and other measures to address
the issues such as wage disparity and workplace safety for women.
To reduce gender wage gap, Government has enacted the Equal
Remuneration Act, 1976, which provides for payment of equal remuneration to
men and women workers for the same work or work of similar nature without any
discrimination and also prevent discrimination against women while making
recruitment for the same work or work of similar nature, or in any condition of
service subsequent to recruitment such as promotion, training or transfer. The
Act is implemented by the Central and State Government in their respective
spheres.
The Code on Wages, 2019, serves as a comprehensive and streamlined
framework for labour laws about wages. This code underscores the principles of
gender equality and nondiscrimination by explicitly prohibiting wage disparity
based on gender. It establishes the principle of equal remuneration for both
women and men engaged in the same work or work of a similar nature, thereby
promoting fairness and equity in the realm of remuneration. The Equal
Remuneration Act, 1976 has been subsumed in the Code on Wages, 2019.
The Factories Act, 1948 specifies safety measures for women workers,
encompassing restrictions on working near cotton openers, prohibition from
cleaning, lubricating, or adjusting any part of a machine while it is in motion, and
not allowing them to lift excessive weights. In addition to these specific
10.02.2025 89
provisions, the Act also mandates generic safety measures applicable to both
women and men workers, including the fencing of machinery near which they
work, regulations for work on or near machinery in motion, and precautions
against hazardous processes.
Government has enacted “the Sexual Harassment of Women at
Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013” (SH Act) to
provide protection to women against sexual harassment at workplace and for the
prevention and redressal of complaints related to it.
The Occupational Safety, Health, and Working Conditions Code
(OSHandWC), 2020, incorporates provisions to address the occupational safety,
health, and welfare of workers including women workers. It emphasizes creating
a secure and healthy working environment, considering the unique health
considerations of women. This code grants women the right to work at night,
subject to their consent, and mandates that employers must establish sufficient
measures to ensure the safety and well-being of female workers during at night.
Also, the code extends its provisions to transportation, mandating employers to
provide a pickup and drop-off service for female employees. The Factories Act,
1948 has been subsumed in the OSHandWC Code, 2020.
Government is also implementing women centric schemes such as
Mission Shakti, Namo Drone Didi, Lakhpati Didi, Women in Science and
Engineering- KIRAN (WISE-KIRAN), SERB-POWER (Promoting Opportunities
for Women in Exploratory Research), Women Industrial Training Institutes,
10.02.2025 90
National Vocational Training Institutes and Regional Vocational Training
Institutes etc. for improving the employability of women.
The Ministry of Labour and Employment in January, 2024 also issued an
“Advisory for Employers to Promote Women Workforce Participation”. This
advisory inter-alia mentions the employers to ensure all precautionary safety
measures for women workers; ensure creation of Internal Complaints
Committees (ICCs) for sexual harassment within organizations; ensure timely
redressal of safety concerns of women workers; ensure creation of awareness
within the enterprise/unit about what constitutes sexual harassment, violence
against women, etc.
DETERIORATING CONDITION OF HERITAGE MONUMENTS
*95. SHRI SUKHDEO BHAGAT:
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state
(a) whether the Government has taken note of several Reports, including
those from Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG), which have
highlighted the deteriorating condition of heritage monuments under the
Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and if so, the details thereof; and
(b) whether it is a fact that despite allocation of more funds by the
Government, there has been no significant improvement in the preservation and
maintenance of heritage sites under ASI and if so, the details along with reasons
therefor?
10.02.2025 91
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) Yes, Sir. The observation/recommendations of several Reports, including
those from Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) have been received
and noted. Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) takes up conservation and
preservation of centrally protected monuments/sites as per National
Conservation Policy, 2014 and provisions of Ancient Monuments and
Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958 (AMASR Act,1958).
(b) ASI takes up conservation, preservation and maintenance of 3,698
centrally protected monuments/sites in the country as per requirement and
availability of resources.
STRENGTHENING OF FINANCIAL INCLUSION SCHEMES
*96. DR. BHOLA SINGH:
SHRI NABA CHARAN MAJHI:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) the total reach and beneficiary count of financial inclusion schemes, including
PM Jan Dhan Yojana, PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana, PM Suraksha Bima
Yojana, Atal Pension Yojana, PM Mudra Yojana, Stand-Up India and PM
Vishwakarma;
(b) the steps taken to ensure accessibility of these schemes to women, rural
population marginalised groups and underprivileged communities in the country,
State-wise;
10.02.2025 92
(c) whether private banks are being encouraged to participate actively in
expanding financial inclusion initiatives;
(d) whether there are any upcoming reforms or digital innovations to improve the
efficiency and transparency of financial inclusion efforts and if so, the steps taken
by the Government to enhance awareness and increase participation in these
schemes in each State of the country, including Maharashtra, Sidhi Lok Sabha
Constituency; and
(e) the way by which the State Authorities address the challenges such as low
enrolment, lack of awareness and implementation gaps in various States of the
country?
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a) to (e): The Government initiated the National Mission for Financial Inclusion
(NMFI), namely the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) in August, 2014
to provide universal banking services for every unbanked household based on
the guiding principles of banking the unbanked, securing the unsecured, funding
the unfunded and serving unserved and underserved areas, with a strong focus
on women. To give further impetus to financial inclusion initiatives of the
Government, PMJDY Scheme was extended beyond 14.08.2018 and the focus
was shifted to “every unbanked adult” instead of “every household”. A total of
54.58 crore Jan-Dhan accounts have been opened till 15.01.2025, of which
30.37 crore (55.7%) belong to women. NMFI has also facilitated the coverage of
10.02.2025 93
women with various social security and credit linked Schemes, the details of
which are given in the enclosed Statement I and II.
To ensure accessibility of these schemes to women, rural population,
marginalised groups and underprivileged communities in the country, various
steps are being taken such as:
1) Allocation of targets to all banks under each scheme;
2) Organisation of various camps and specialised campaigns to
promote awareness;
3) Periodic review of performance of banks etc;
All Banks, including private banks, participate in these activities to ensure
effectiveness of these schemes and make them accessible to all stakeholders.
Several initiatives are being undertaken in an ongoing manner by the
Government along with State Authorities to address challenges such as low
enrolment, lack of awareness, etc. being faced in financial inclusion schemes in
the country. Some of these are as under:
i. Several special campaigns have been launched, from time to time, at Gram
Panchayat level to reach the last mile beneficiaries. These campaigns aim
at enrolling individuals under Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
(PMJJBY), Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) and other
financial inclusion Schemes.
ii. The State Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC) plays a crucial role by
coordinating efforts among Banks, Government agencies, Lead District
10.02.2025 94
Managers, Financial Institutions, Insurance companies, and other
stakeholders to increase coverage under these Schemes at the state level.
iii. Centre for Financial Literacy (CFL) pilot project on financial literacy was
initiated by the Reserve Bank of India in 2017 with an objective to adopt
community led innovative and participatory approaches to financial literacy.
iv. A strong network of about 13 lakh Banking Correspondents (BCs),
representing the last mile connect in the Banking Services delivery system,
is also enrolling eligible people under financial inclusion Schemes.
v. To make digital financial services more accessible and user-friendly, 107
Digital Banking Units (DBUs) have been set-up by Banks (as on December
2024) with an objective to ensure the benefits of digital banking to every
nook and corner of the country. These units offer facilities like opening of
saving bank accounts, passbook printing, transfer of funds, loan
applications, etc.
vi. Further, various online platforms like Jan Samarth portal, PSB Loans in 59
Minutes, Stand-up Mitra, etc., have been established to provide quick and
hassle-free credit to everyone in a user-friendly manner.

NEW KVs AND JNVs
*99. DR. KALANIDHI VEERASWAMY:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government proposes to open new Kendriya Vidyalayas and
Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas across the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof, State-wise including Tamil Nadu;
(c) the States where these new schools are being established;
(d) the expected timeline for the completion of construction and
commencement of operation in these schools;
(e) the steps taken by the Government to ensure adequate infrastructure,
teaching staff, and resources for these new schools; and
(f) the expected impact of these new schools on the education system and
access to quality education?
10.02.2025 108
THE MINISTER OF EDUCATION (SHRI DHARMENDRA PRADHAN):
(a) to (c): At present, 1253 Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs) and 653 Navodaya
Vidyalayas (NVs) are functional across the country. Opening of new KVs and
NVs is a continuous process.
KVs are opened primarily to cater to the educational needs of the wards of
transferable Central Government Employees including Defence and Para-
military personnel, Central Autonomous Bodies, Central Public Sector
Undertakings (PSUs) and Central Institute of Higher Learning (IHL) by providing
a common programme of education throughout the country. Proposals for
opening of new KVs may be sponsored by Ministries or Departments of the
Government of India / State Governments / Union Territories (UTs)
Administrations, with commitment to provide requisite resources including land
and temporary accommodation for setting up a new KV as per norms. The
proposals are subject to approval of the competent authority as per extant
procedures. The Government of India has recently approved the proposal for
opening of 85 new Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs) under Civil/Defence Sector across
the country and expansion of one existing KV i.e. KV Shivamogga, District
Shivamogga, Karnataka by adding 2 additional Sections in all the classes
including 02 KVs in Tamil Nadu (Theni, district Theni and Pillaiyarpatti, district
Thanjavur).
The Navodaya Vidyalaya Scheme envisages for opening of one NV in each
district of the country. With sanctioning of 62 new NVs in November 2016, all 638
10.02.2025 109
districts (as on 31st May, 2014) of the States/UTs which have accepted the
scheme, other than 6 districts with 100% urban population, have been covered
under this scheme. The State of Tamilnadu has yet to accept the Navodaya
Vidyalaya Scheme. Opening of new NVs depends on the willingness of the
concerned State Government/UT administration to make available requisite
suitable land, free of cost, for construction of the permanent building and making
available required temporary building, free of rent, to run the Vidyalaya till the
permanent building is constructed. Sanction and opening of new NVs depends
on approval by the competent authority as per extant procedures. Further,
Government of India has recently sanctioned new NVs in 28 newly created/
carved out districts.
(d): Construction of permanent buildings for KVs and NVs is a continuous
process, which depends upon identification of suitable land, completion of
transfer / lease formalities in favour of Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan (KVS) and
Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti (NVS) by the sponsoring authorities, submission of
drawings / estimates by construction agency, availability of funds and requisite
approvals etc.
(e): All new KVs and NVs buildings are equipped with all required infrastructure
as per KVS and NVS norms viz. classrooms, library, computer lab, physics lab,
chemistry lab, biology lab, maths lab, smart classrooms etc. and sports facilities
to ensure quality education to the students. In addition, dormitories, mess,
multipurpose halls / central covered courtyard etc. are made available in NVs as
10.02.2025 110
per norms. Further, teaching and non-teaching staff are sanctioned as per KVS
and NVS norms after opening of the Vidyalaya.
(f): Establishment of these 85 new KVs and expansion of one existing KV and
28 new NVs would additionally benefit approximately 82560 students in these
KVs and approximately 15680 students in these NVs across the country to get
opportunity to avail of affordable and quality modern education.
AMOUNT ALLOCATED BY COMPANIES UNDER CSR IN ANDHRA
PRADESH
*100. SHRI PUTTA MAHESH KUMAR:
Will the Minister of CORPORATE AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the total number of companies registered, presently functional, dormant
and struck off during the last five years across Andhra Pradesh, district-wise,
especially in Eluru district along with their details;
(b) the list of CSR initiatives undertaken and beneficiaries of the same at
present in Andhra Pradesh, district-wise, especially in Eluru district;
(c) the total funds allocated, sector-wise by companies for CSR activities in
Andhra Pradesh during the last five years, district-wise, especially in Eluru
district;
(d) whether the Government has considered increasing the financial criteria of
allocating 2% of average net profits of companies; and
(e) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
10.02.2025 111
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a): The details of total number of companies registered, presently functional,
dormant and struck off during the last five years across Andhra Pradesh including
Eluru district are given in the enclosed Statement-I.
(b): On the basis of the filings made by the companies in the MCA21 registry,
district-wise CSR expenditure in Andhra Pradesh during last three Financial
Years (FY) from 2018-19 to 2022-23 are given in the enclosed Statement–II.
The beneficiary-wise details of CSR expenditure is not maintained.
(c): There is no provision of allocation of CSR funds under CSR legal framework.
However, on the basis of the filings made by the companies in the MCA21
registry, sector-wise CSR expenditure in Andhra Pradesh during the last five
financial years (FYs) from 2018-19 to 2022-23 is are given in the enclosed
Statement-III.
(d) and (e): No such proposal has been considered by the Government as the
extant provisions are considered appropriate.
10.02.2025 112

(ii) Unstarred Question Nos. 921 to 1150
िव(cid:176)ीय िश(cid:177)ा सबं ंधी काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म

(क) और (ख): िविनधानकता(cid:170) िश(cid:177)ा और संर(cid:177)ण िनिध (cid:255)ािधकरण (आईईपीएफए) (cid:300)ारा छ(cid:176)ीसगढ़
रा(cid:186)य सिहत पूर े देश म(cid:164) िनवेशक जाग(cid:322)कता काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (आईएपी) चलाए जा रहे ह(cid:167)। इन िनवेशक
जाग(cid:322)कता काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म(cid:332) के अंतग(cid:170)त, िशिवर(cid:332) का आयोजन करके िव(cid:176)ीय सा(cid:177)रता और जाग(cid:322)कता
(cid:255)दान क(cid:236) जाती है। "िनवेशक दीदी" भारतीय डाक भुगतान ब(cid:167)क(cid:332) (आईपीपीबी) के मा(cid:197)यम स े क(cid:236) गई
ऐसी अिभनव पहल(cid:332) म(cid:164) से एक पहल है, िजसम(cid:164) (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त मिहला डािकय(cid:332) को िशिवर आयोिजत करने
के िलए िनयोिजत िकया जाता है, जो िक वंिचत मिहलाओ ं को लि(cid:177)त करता है। इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:265),
आईईपीएफए छ(cid:176)ीसगढ़ सिहत देश भर म(cid:164) 2022-23 से िनवेशक जाग(cid:322)कता काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (आईएपी)
आयोिजत करने के िलए भारतीय सनदी लेखाकार सं(cid:214)थान (आईसीएआई) के साथ भी सहयोग कर
रहा है।
िव(cid:176)ीय समावेशन के िलए रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय काय(cid:170)नीित के अंतग(cid:170)त, भारतीय (cid:229)रजव(cid:170) ब(cid:167)क (आरबीआई)
ने छ(cid:176)ीसगढ़ रा(cid:186)य सिहत देश भर म(cid:164) िजला (cid:214)तर पर अ(cid:250)णी ब(cid:167)क(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा िव(cid:176)ीय सा(cid:177)रता के (cid:198)(cid:254)(cid:332)
(एफएलसी) और (cid:202)लॉक (cid:214)तर पर िव(cid:176)ीय सा(cid:177)रता के (cid:198)(cid:254)(cid:332) (सीएफएल) क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना के मा(cid:197)यम से
िव(cid:176)ीय सा(cid:177)रता के िलए िदशा-िनद(cid:165)श जारी िकए ह(cid:167)। इन िदशा-िनद(cid:165)श(cid:332) के अनसु ार, ब(cid:167)क िनयिमत (cid:322)प

10.02.2025 120
ADMISSION IN PHD COURSES OF IITs
922. SHRI S. VENKATESAN:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
the total numbers of students who got admission in PhD courses of IITs
with the details of SC/ST/OBC among them in the academic year 2024-25,
department-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH
EASTERN REGION (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
IITs are autonomous institutes governed by the Institutes of Technology
Act, 1961 and the Statutes framed there under from time to time. Government
has mandated IITs to comply with the statutory provisions of the Central
Education Institutes (Reservation in Admission) Act, 2006, as amended from
time to time, for admission of reserved category candidates to various
programmes including PhD. Besides the Statutes, the IITs duly follow all the
guidelines, norms and directions issued by the Government of India from time to
time.
The total numbers of students admitted in PhD courses of IITs in the
academic year 2024-25 is 6210 out of which, the number of SC/ST/OBC students
is 2484.

कॉरपोरटे काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी
((cid:174)ी हष(cid:170) म(cid:208)हो(cid:253)ा):
(क): सीएसआर िविधक ढांचे के अंतग(cid:170)त सीएसआर िनिधय(cid:332) के आबंटन का कोई (cid:255)ावधान नह(cid:233) है।
कारपोरेट सामािजक दािय(cid:194)व (सीएसआर) के िलए कानूनी ढांचा कं पनी अिधिनयम, 2013
(‘अिधिनयम’) क(cid:236) धारा 135, अिधिनयम क(cid:236) अनसु ूची VII और कं पनी (सीएसआर नीित) िनयम,
2014 के तहत (cid:255)दान िकया गया है। अिधिनयम के तहत, (cid:255)(cid:194)येक सीएसआर अिधदेिशत कं पनी को
एक सीएसआर सिमित का गठन करना होगा। सिमित सीएसआर नीित तैयार करती है और उसक(cid:236)
िसफा(cid:229)रश करती है और कं पनी का बोड(cid:170) कं पनी के सीएसआर काय(cid:170)कलाप(cid:332) क(cid:236) योजना बनाता, उन
पर िनण(cid:170)य लेता, उनका िन(cid:213)पादन करता है और उन पर िनगरानी करता है।
(ख): कं पिनय(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा एमसीए21 रिज(cid:214)(cid:367)ी म (cid:164) फ़ाइल िकए गए सीएसआर से संबंिधत सभी आंकड़े
साव(cid:170)जिनक (cid:322)प से उपल(cid:202)ध ह(cid:167) और इ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) www.csr.gov.in पर देखा जा सकता है। सीएसआर
अिधदेिशत कं पिनय(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा वािष(cid:170)क फाइिलंग के आधार पर, िपछले तीन िव(cid:176)ीय वष(cid:334) (िव.व.) अथा(cid:170)त
2020-21, 2021-22 और 2022-23 के दौरान आकां(cid:177)ी िजल(cid:332) म(cid:164) सीएसआर के तहत रा(cid:186)यवार,
िजला-वार और िवकास (cid:177)(cid:253)े -वार खच(cid:170) क(cid:236) गई रािश (cid:248)मशः िववरण-I, िववरण-II और िववरण-III के
(cid:322)प म(cid:164) संल(cid:181)न है।
(ग) और (घ): चूंिक कारपोरेट सामािजक दािय(cid:194)व काय(cid:170)कलाप का (cid:255)ारभं , (cid:255)बंधन और िनगरानी िकसी

सीएसआर नीित को अनुमोिदत करना अपेि(cid:177)त है। कं पनी (सीएसआर नीित) िनयम, 2014 के िनयम
4 (5) के तहत कं पनी के बोड(cid:170) को (cid:214)वयं को संत(cid:310)ु करना होता है िक इस (cid:255)कार िवत(cid:229)रत धन का
उपयोग इसके (cid:300)ारा अनुमोिदत उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य(cid:332) के िलए और तरीके स े िकया गया है, और म(cid:180)ु य िव(cid:176)ीय

10.02.2025 134
EXEMPTED CATEGORY OF LAND UNDER FOREST (CONSERVATION)
AMENDMENT BILL, 2023
925. SHRI RAJA RAM SINGH:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE
be pleased to state:
(a) whether under the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2023, States
land situated within 100 km along the international borders, Line of Control or
Line of Actual Control, proposed to be used for construction of strategic linear
project for national importance or security, if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether any Committee has been formed to decide on the benchmark of
100 km, if so, the recommendations of the said Committee;
(c) whether in context of this Policy has the Government drawn any plans to
protect the environment of Himalayan States; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) to (d): The Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2023, passed by the
Parliament, inter-alia provided that such forest land, as is situated within a
distance of hundred kilometres along international borders or Line of Control or
Line of Actual Control, as the case may be, proposed to be used for construction
of strategic linear project of national importance and concerning national
security, shall not be covered under the provisions of Van (Sanrakshan Evam
10.02.2025 135
Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980. The Forest (Conservation) Amendment Act,
2023 has been notified on 04.08.2023 and subsequently enforced by the Central
Government w.e.f. 01.12.2023.
The extent of 100 km has been decided in consultation with the ministries
and stakeholders concerned. Further, in accordance with the provisions of
section 1A(3) of the Adhiniyam, the Central Government has issued guidelines
on 29.11.2023 and 24.09.2024 specifying the terms and conditions to be abided
by the States and Union territories while considering exemptions provided under
section 1A(2) of the Adhiniyam.
DEVELOPMENT OF SUSTAINABLE WILDLIFE AND FOREST TOURISM
926. SHRI NAVASKANI K.:
SHRI C. N. ANNADURAI:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is implementing any scheme to promote wildlife and
forest tourism in the country and if so, the details thereof alongwith the scope
of the same in the State of Tamil Nadu;
(b) the steps taken by the Government to tap the potential and promotion of the
wildlife and forest tourism in the notified Tiger Reserves, National Parks and
Wildlife Sanctuaries within the country including said State;
10.02.2025 136
(c) whether any scheme has been put in place to involve local communities as
stakeholders in the development of sustainable wildlife and forest tourism; and
(d) if so, the details thereof along with the funds provided by the Government for
promoting sustainable wildlife and forest tourism during the last three years,
State/UT-wise including Tamil Nadu?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) to (d): The Ministry provides financial assistance to States/UTs for
conservation of wildlife and its habitats including for undertaking eco-tourism
activities under the Centrally Sponsored schemes – ‘Development of Wildlife
Habitats’, ‘Project Tiger and Elephant’ including in the State of Tamil Nadu.
The tourism activities in and around tiger reserves are carried out as per
the National Tiger Conservation Authority (Normative Standards for Tourism
activities and Project Tiger) Guidelines, 2012 issued by the Ministry under
section 38-O(1)(c) of the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972.
The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has also issued
‘Guidelines for Sustainable Ecotourism in Forest and Wildlife Aeas-2021’ which
promotes the engagement of local communities in nature tourism in a manner
that enriches local economy and encourages sustainable use of indigenous
materials through financially viable values chains.
10.02.2025 137
The details of financial assistance provided to States/Union Territories
under the Centrally Sponsored Schemes – ‘Development of Wildlife Habitat’ and
Project Tiger and Elephant’ are given in the enclosed Statement-I and
Statement-II respectively.

FUNDS ALLOCATED/UTILIZED UNDER SPORTS AND PHYSICAL
EDUCATION (PE) COMPONENT OF SSA
927. SHRI BAJRANG MANOHAR SONWANE:
SHRI BHASKAR MURLIDHAR BHAGARE:
SHRI NILESH DNYANDEV LANKE:
SHRIMATI SUPRIYA SULE:
DR. AMOL RAMSING KOLHE:
PROF. VARSHA EKNATH GAIKWAD:
SHRI SANJAY DINA PATIL:
SHRI DHAIRYASHEEL RAJSINH MOHITE PATIL:

(a) the details of funds allocated and utilized under the Sports and Physical

years, year-wise in the State of Maharashtra;
(b) whether funds under this component have been utilized for the development
of sports infrastructure in schools, if so, the details of sports infrastructure created
or upgraded during the last three years;
(c) the criteria fixed for the selection of schools for infrastructure development
under the scheme;
(d) whether special provisions have been made for this component to promote
sports and physical education in rural and tribal schools and if so, the details

(e) the steps taken by the Government to bridge the gap between rural/tribal and
urban schools in terms of sports infrastructure;
10.02.2025 141
(f) the details of funds allocated for the training and capacity-building of physical
education teachers under the scheme and the number of such teachers trained
during the last three years; and
(g) whether the Government has conducted any assessment of the outcomes of
the Sports and Physical Education component of the Samagra Shiksha
Abhiyaan, if so, the details of the findings, particularly in improving students’
participation in sports?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT
AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY
OF EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b): During last five years the funds allocated and utilized by the State of
Maharashtra under the Sports and physical Education component is as below:

2019-2020 4711.15 0
2020-2021 0 0
2021-2022 4681.4 0
2022-2023 4682.8 14.95
2023-2024 4677.55 386.14
Source : PRABNDH
(c) to (g): Education is in the concurrent list of the Constitution and majority of the
schools are under the domain of respective State and UT Governments. The
Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Samagra Shiksha is implemented in partnership
with all the States and UTs and financial assistance is provided to all States and
UTs for various components.
10.02.2025 142
The Department of School Education and Literacy is implementing Integrated
Centrally Sponsored Scheme for School Education- Samagra Shiksha. The scheme
has been redesigned and aligned with the recommendations of the National
Education Policy, 2020. It aims to ensure that all children have access to quality
education with an equitable and inclusive classroom environment irrespective of
region and category. As per norms of the scheme, funds are released based on the
annual plans prepared by the States and UTs as per their requirements/ priority
including infrastructure development and reflected in their respective Annual Work
Plan and Budget (AWPandB) proposals which are then appraised and
approved/estimated by the Project Approval Board (PAB) in the Department of
School Education and Literacy. The scheme includes the Sports and Physical
Education component under which provision for grants for sports equipment for
indoor and outdoor games in all government schools has been made. A provision
has been made for Government schools for grant for sports equipment at the rate
of Rs. 5000 for Primary Schools, Rs. 10,000 for upper primary schools and up to
Rs. 25,000 for secondary and senior secondary schools per annum. In addition, as
per new norms of the scheme, an additional grant upto Rs. 25000 per school is
provided to schools in case at least 2 students of that school win the medal in Khelo
India National school games competition.
The Scheme also provides annual recurring school composite grant for all
Government Schools for the replacement of non-functional school equipment and
for incurring other recurring costs such as consumables for play materials, sports
10.02.2025 143
equipment, organizing sports events, laboratories, electricity charges, internet,
water, teaching aids etc. The amount of grant varies from Rs. 25,000 to Rs.
1,00,000 per annum depending upon the number of students in the school. State
and UTs are also advised to set aside a certain amount from the school grant for
these activities.
This Department had issued Guidelines for Sports Grant under Samagra
Shiksha in December, 2018 which were revised incorporating the recommendation
of NEP, 2020 in August, 2023. These guidelines highlighted proper utilization of
funds, promotion of sports activities and maintenance of sports
infrastructure/playground in schools of sports infrastructure/playground in schools.
(https://samagra.education.gov.in/docs/revised_samagra_sports.pdf).
To diagnose outcomes and determine necessary interventions in policies,
teaching practices and learning, the Government conducts various assessments
based on different parameters. As part of the initiatives undertaken by the
Department, students across the country actively participate in sports-related
activities in schools. The Department has observed an unprecedented participation
of over 8.28 Crore individuals from the school community on the International Day
of Yoga, 2024. On the occasion of the 4th anniversary of the National Education
Policy (NEP), the Department celebrated ‘Shiksha Saptah,’ from 22nd July to 28th
July, 2024 dedicating 3rd day as Sports Day to promote indigenous games in
schools, where 4.48 Crore students and teachers participated nationwide.
Additionally, the Department is implementing various programs such as Khelo India,
10.02.2025 144
Fit India, and Football for Schools (F4S) to engage students in sports activities in
schools, in collaboration with the Department of Sports.
PROCEEDS FROM DISINVESTMENT
928. SHRI KRISHNA PRASAD TENNETI:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) the target set for proceeds from disinvestment by the Government in the FY
2024-25 and 2025-26 including the details of the organisations for the same;
(b) the actual proceeds received from disinvestment by the Government in the
FY 2024-25 including sources of such proceeds; and
(c) the reasons for a mismatch between the target set and actual proceeds from
disinvestment, if any?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE (SHRI PANKAJ
CHAUDHARY):
(a): Fixing separate disinvestment target/estimate has been discontinued since
the Revised Estimate (RE) stage of FY 2023-24. During FY 2024-25, there is no
separate estimate/target for disinvestment receipts. Rs 50,000 crore was kept
under Miscellaneous Capital Receipts at Budget Estimate (BE) stage of FY2024-
25 which has been revised to Rs 33,000 crore at RE stage. Miscellaneous
Capital Receipts includes estimated receipts on account of management of
equity investments and public assets through various mechanisms. Budget
Estimate for Miscellaneous Capital Receipts (MCR) for FY 2025-26 has been
kept at Rs 47,000 crore.
10.02.2025 145
(b): Disinvestment/Asset Monetisation is an ongoing process, and
execution/completion of specific transactions hinges upon market conditions,
domestic and global economic outlook, geopolitical factors, investor interest and
administrative feasibility. During the current Financial Year 2024-25,
Government has realised Rs 8,625.05 crore as disinvestment proceeds through
various disinvestment transactions (as on 04.02.2025). Details are as under:
S. No Name of the Entity Method of Actual
disinvestment proceeds
/others (₹ Crore)

of India (GIC)
2. Cochin Shipyard Limited Offer for Sale 2,015.32

highest bid of M/s. Konoike Transport Co. Ltd. for sale of 100% shares of FSNL
held by MSTC Ltd. at the equity value of Rs. 320 crore was accepted by the
Government. The proceeds have accrued to MSTC Ltd and not to
Government.
(c): Question of mismatch doesn’t arise as there is no separate/specific target set
for disinvestment proceeds since 2023-24.

िववरण-II
मिहला उ(cid:299)िमय(cid:332) के िलए भारत सरकार के अ(cid:198)य म(cid:253)ं ालय(cid:332)/िवभाग(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत क(cid:236) जा रही
िविभ(cid:198)न पहल(cid:332) का िववरण इस (cid:255)कार है:
1. वािण(cid:186)य एवं उ(cid:299)ोग म(cid:253)ं ालय के अंतग(cid:170)त उ(cid:299)ोग एवं आतं (cid:229)रक (cid:211)यापार संवध(cid:170)न िवभाग
(डीपीआईआईटी) - नवाचार को बढ़ावा देने और िनवेश को (cid:255)ो(cid:194)सािहत करने के िलए एक मजबूत
इकोिस(cid:214)टम बनाने के उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य से सरकार ने 16 जनवरी 2016 को (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप इंिडया पहल श(cid:322)ु क(cid:236)।
जीएसआर अिधसूचना 127 (ई) िदनांक 19 फरवरी 2019 के तहत िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत पा(cid:253)ता शत(cid:334) के अनुसार,
उ(cid:299)ोग एवं आंत(cid:229)रक (cid:211)यापार संवध(cid:170)न िवभाग (डीपीआईआईटी) (cid:300)ारा (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप इंिडया पहल के तहत
स(cid:214)ं थाओ ंको '(cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप' के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) मा(cid:198)यता दी जाती है। 31 िदसंबर 2024 तक, 1,57,706 स(cid:214)ं थाओ ं
को डीपीआईआईटी (cid:300)ारा (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप के (cid:322)प म (cid:164) मा(cid:198)यता दी गई ह,ै िजनम(cid:164) से 75,935 स(cid:214)ं थाओ ंने (cid:214)वयं
(cid:229)रपोट(cid:170) िकया है िक उनके पास कम से कम एक मिहला िनदेशक/भागीदार ह(cid:167)। डीपीआईआईटी (cid:300)ारा (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-
अप के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) मा(cid:198)यता (cid:255)ा(cid:302) 75,935 सं(cid:214)थाओ ंम (cid:164) स,े िज(cid:198)ह(cid:332)ने (cid:214)वयं (cid:229)रपोट(cid:170) िकया है िक उनके पास कम
से कम एक मिहला िनदेशक/भागीदार ह(cid:167), 34 दादरा और नगर हवेली तथा दमन और दीव से ह(cid:167), 2,396
म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश से ह(cid:167) और 13,947 महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) से ह(cid:167)।
इसके अलावा, सरकार देश भर म(cid:164) मिहला उ(cid:299)मशीलता को बढ़ावा देने के िलए (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप इंिडया पहल
के अंतग(cid:170)त िविश(cid:310) पहल/काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रही है। ऐसी पहल(cid:332) का िववरण इस (cid:255)कार है:
i. मिहला-नीत वाले (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)अ(cid:200)स को इि(cid:179)वटी और ऋण दोन(cid:332) के (cid:255)वाह को बढ़ावा देने के िलए, भारतीय लघु
उ(cid:299)ोग िवकास ब(cid:167)क (िसडबी) (cid:300)ारा संचािलत (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अ(cid:200)स के िलए फं ड ऑफ फं ड्स योजना म(cid:164) िनिध का
10% मिहला-नीत वाले (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अ(cid:200)स के िलए आरि(cid:177)त है।
ii. मिहला-नीत वाले वैकि(cid:208)पक िनवेश कोष (एआईएफ) उ(cid:184)च (cid:214)तर के (cid:255)बंधन श(cid:208)ु क (0.1% (cid:255)ित वष(cid:170)) के

स(cid:214)ं थान संिवतरण/शेष रािश का 1.5% (cid:255)ित वष(cid:170) क(cid:236) मानक दर का भगु तान करता है।
iv. मिहला (cid:177)मता िवकास काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (िवंग) मिहला-नीत वाले (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप के िलए एक अनूठा (cid:177)मता िवकास
काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म है, िजसका उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य मह(cid:194)वाका(cid:177)ं ी और (cid:214)थािपत मिहला उ(cid:299)िमय(cid:332) को उनक(cid:236) (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)-अप या(cid:253)ा म (cid:164)
पहचानना और उनका समथ(cid:170)न करना है। काय(cid:170)शालाए ँ तकनीक, िनमा(cid:170)ण, उ(cid:194)पाद, मशीन, खा(cid:299), कृिष,

एचईड(cid:202)(cid:208)यू और 742 िजला संक(cid:208)प: एचईड(cid:202)(cid:208)यू ि(cid:248)याशील ह(cid:167)।
4. िकसान क(cid:208)याण िवभाग िकसान(cid:332) और कृ िष उ(cid:299)िमय(cid:332), िजनम(cid:164) मिहला कृिष उ(cid:299)मी भी शािमल ह(cid:167), के
िलए िविभ(cid:198)न योजनाओ ं और काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म(cid:332) को काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रहा है। भारत सरकार वष(cid:170) 2018-19 से
रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय कृिष िवकास योजना (आरके वीवाई) के अंतग(cid:170)त "नवाचार और कृिष-उ(cid:299)मशीलता िवकास"
काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म को काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रही है, िजसका उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य दशे म(cid:164) िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता (cid:255)दान करके और एक
इ(cid:179)ं यूबेशन इकोिस(cid:214)टम का पोषण करके नवाचार और कृिष-उ(cid:299)मशीलता को बढ़ावा देना है।
कृिष-ि(cid:179)लिनक और कृिष (cid:211)यवसाय क(cid:164) (cid:254) योजना (एसी और एबीसी) अ(cid:255)ैल, 2002 से काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत
क(cid:236) जा रही है। इस योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य कृिष िवकास का समथ(cid:170)न करने और रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय कृिष िव(cid:214)तार (cid:255)बंधन

स(cid:214)ं थान (मैनेज) और नाबाड(cid:170) के सहयोग से साव(cid:170)जिनक िव(cid:214)तार के (cid:255)यास(cid:332) को पूरक बनाने के िलए कृिष

(cid:255)ा(cid:302) कर सकते ह(cid:167)। यह योजना एफ़पीओ, एसएचजी, सहकारी सिमितय(cid:332) या सू(cid:224)म खा(cid:299) (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण उ(cid:299)म(cid:332)

लाभािथ(cid:170)य(cid:332) को लाभ िमला।
8. सू(cid:224)म, लघु और म(cid:197)यम उ(cid:299)म म(cid:253)ं ालय (एमएसएमई) – उ(cid:299)मशीलता और कौशल िवकास काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म

काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य एससी/एसटी मिहलाओ,ं शारी(cid:229)रक (cid:322)प से िद(cid:211)यांग, पूव(cid:170) सैिनक(cid:332) और गरीबी रखे ा स े

SCHEMES FOR DEVELOPMENT OF SPORTS IN THE COUNTRY
930. SHRIMATI RACHNA BANERJEE:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
(a) the number of schemes running by the Government in the country for the
development of various sports and youth affairs;
(b) the name and details of the Schemes; and
(c) the details of funds allocated under each Schemes, Scheme and State-
wise?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF
YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
(a) and (b): The Government runs the following major schemes for the
development of sports and youth affairs in the country:
The schemes under the Department of Youth Affairs are:
10.02.2025 161
(i) Rashtriya Yuva Sashaktikaran Karyakram (RYSK) is an umbrella scheme
with the following 7 sub-schemes:
1. Nehru Yuva Kendra Sangathan.
2. National Youth Corps.
3. National Programme for Youth and Adolescent Development.
4. International Cooperation.
5. Youth Hostels.
6. Assistance to Scouting and Guiding Organisations.
7. National Young Leaders Programme.
(ii) National Service Scheme (NSS)
(iii) Rajiv Gandhi National Institute of Youth Development (RGNIYD)
(iv) Mera Yuva Bharat (MY Bharat)
The schemes under the Department of Sports are:
(i) Khelo India- National Programme for Development of Sports
(ii) Assistance to National Sports Federations (NSFs)
(iii) Cash Awards to Medal Winners in International Sports Events and their
Coaches
(iv) National Sports Awards
(v) Pension to Meritorious Sportspersons
(vi) Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National Sports Welfare Scheme
(vii) National Sports Development Fund
(viii) Running Sports Training Centres through Sports Authority of India (SAI)
10.02.2025 162
The details of the above schemes are available in public domain and can
be seen at https://yas.gov.in.
(c): Funds are allocated by this Ministry Scheme-wise, not Sate-wise. The details
of the funds allocated for FY 2024-25 across various schemes of Department of
Youth Affairs and Department of Sports is given in the enclosed Statement-I and
Statement-II respectively.
