Further, Government is providing additional support to athletes from rural areas to
pursue sports as a career by providing various direct/indirect financial support
through various schemes/programmes as under:
10.02.2025 807
I. Under the component “Khelo India Centres and Sports Academies” of Khelo
India Scheme the talent identified under Khelo India Scheme are given options to
join the accredited Khelo India academies and also provided financial assistance
@ Rs. 6.28 Lacs per annum [including Rs 1.20 Lacs as Out of Pocket Allowance
(OPA)] towards training expenses, coaching, competitions exposure, education,
equipment support, scientific support and etc. Also, under the Khelo India Centre
vertical of Khelo India Scheme, Past Champion Athletes (PCAs) are hired as
coaches/mentors for young athletes in Khelo India Centres (KICs) who train these
athletes as well as run the KIC in an autonomous manner or with the support of the
State/UT Sports Department.
II. Under the Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS), Government provides
assistance to India’s top athletes for their preparations for Olympic and Paralympic
Games. Selected athletes are supported with funding from National Sports
Development Fund (NSDF) for customized training and other support not available
under normal schemes of the Ministry. Out of pocket allowance (OPA) is paid @
Rs. 50,000/- per month to Core group athletes. Apart from OPA, entire expenditure
for the training plan submitted by the sportsperson, which is considered and
approved by Mission Olympic Cell (MOC), is met under TOPS.
III. Under the scheme of Assistance to National Sports Federations (ANSF)
financial assistance are given to National Sports Federations (NSFs) for the training
of athletes, which includes all requisite support for training, participation in
International events, conduct of National Championships, conduct of International
10.02.2025 808
tournaments in India, engagement of Foreign Coaches/support staff, scientific and
medical support etc.
IV. Under the Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National Welfare Fund for
Sportspersons (PDUNWFS) Scheme, Government provides direct financial
assistance (upto Rs.2.50 lakh) to sportspersons living in indigent circumstances for
training, procurement for sports equipment, participation in national and
international sports events, etc.
V. Government provides financial support to the sportspersons after their
retirement from active sports through the scheme of Pension to Meritorious
Sportspersons, with the objective of providing an assured monthly income through
annuity for outstanding sportspersons. Under the existing scheme, monthly pension
ranging from Rs. 12,000/- to Rs. 20,000/- is provided to eligible ex-sportspersons.
VI. Under the scheme of Cash Awards to Medal Winners in International Sports
Events and their Coaches, Government provides cash award to outstanding
sportspersons to encourage and motivate them for higher achievements and to act
as inspiring role models for attracting the younger generation to take up sports.
Under this scheme, cash award ranging from Rs. 20,000/- to Rs.75,00,000/- are
granted to sportsperson who have won medals at international events.
VII. Apart from the above schemes, in order to honour athletes/ sportspersons
winning medals in international events, Government also confers the National
Sports Awards every year under various categories like Major Dhyan Chand Khel
Ratna Award, Arjuna Award, Dronacharya Award and Dhyan Chand Award.
10.02.2025 809
(d) No such initiative such as Public-Private Partnerships have been taken in this
Ministry.
LOW GER AT SECONDARY LEVEL SCHOOLS IN RAMPUR, U.P.
1106. SHRI MOHIBBULLAH:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the low Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) at
the secondary level in Rampur district of Uttar Pradesh;
(b) if so, the initiatives being undertaken by the Government to address the low
Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) in the above district;
(c) the details of the secondary and senior secondary schools upgraded during the
last five years in the above district; and
(d) the details of school-wise enrollment and teacher availability in Government and
Government-aided secondary and senior secondary schools in the district that have
been upgraded during the last five years?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) The Department of School Education and Literacy maintains Unified District
Information System for Education Plus (U-DISE+) as Management Information
Systems(MIS). U-DISE+ collects information from all the schools (government,
aided and private) annually on various educational indicators including Gross
Enrolment Ratio (GER) across all States and UTs.
10.02.2025 810
GER is the total enrolment in a specified level of school education, regardless
of age, expressed as a percentage of the population in the official age group
corresponding to that level in a given school year. The population figures used for
the calculation are derived from the Report of the Technical Group on Population
Projections (TGPP-July 2020), published by the Ministry of Health and Family
Welfare (MoHFW).
Since the calculation requires population as the denominator, and the Report
of the TGPP does not provide district-wise population estimates, therefore the
district-wise GER is not maintained in the UDISE +.
(b) This Department is implementing an integrated centrally sponsored scheme for
School education- Samagra Shiksha. The scheme treats school education
holistically, without segmentation from pre-primary to class XII and has been
aligned with the recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
The Scheme aims to ensure that all children have access to quality education with
an equitable and inclusive classroom environment which should take care of their
diverse background, multilingual needs, different academic abilities and make them
active participants in the learning process.
Under Samagra Shiksha, financial assistance is provided to States and UTs
for undertaking various activities for Universalization of School Education including
increasing Gross Enrolment Ratio which includes opening/strengthening of new
schools upto senior secondary level, construction of school buildings and additional
classrooms, development/strengthening of school infrastructure in northern border
areas under Vibrant Village Programme, setting up, up-gradation and running of
10.02.2025 811
Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas, construction of hostels for PVTGs under PM-
JANMAN , setting up of Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Avasiya Vidyalayas,
construction of hostels under Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan for
unsaturated ST population, free uniforms to eligible children and free text books at
elementary level, transport allowance and undertaking enrolment and retention
drives. Special training for age appropriate admission of out of school children and
residential as well as non-residential training for older children, seasonal hostels /
residential camps, special training centres at worksites, transport/ escort facility are
also supported to bring out of school children to the formal schooling system
including support to complete their education through NIOS/SIOS. Under the
student oriented component for the children with special needs, financial assistance
is provided for identification and assessment of children with special needs, aids
and appliances, braille kits and books, appropriate teaching learning material and
stipend to girl students with disability etc.
(b) and (d) As per norms of Samagra Shiksha, annual plans are prepared by the
States and UTs as per their requirements/ priority (including upgradation of
schools) and the same is reflected in their respective Annual Work Plan and Budget
(AWPandB) Proposals. These plans are then appraised and approved by the
Project Approval Board (PAB) in the Department of School Education and Literacy
in consultation with the States and UTs as per the programmatic and financial
norms of the scheme and physical and financial progress of the State for the
interventions approved earlier. No proposal for upgradation of school(s) in Rampur
district of Uttar Pradesh during the period 2020-21 to 2024-25 has been received.
10.02.2025 812
SETTING UP OF INDIAN INSTITUTE OF SKILLS IN THE COUNTRY
1107. DR. PRABHA MALLIKARJUN:
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government proposes to set up the Indian Institute of Skills (IIS) in
the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof, State-wise including Karnataka;
(c) the details of the funds allocated/sanctioned for the institutes;
(d) whether the Government identified the locations for said institute in the
Davanagere Constituency;
(e) if so, the details thereof;
(f) whether the Government is planning to set up these institutes under PPP model;
and
(g) if so, the details and the time by which these will likely to be set up?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (g): The Government of India approved setting up of Indian Institute of Skills
(IISs) in PPP model at three locations in the country viz. Mumbai, Kanpur and
Ahmedabad. These institutes are conceptualized to bring in the best in class
infrastructure and facilities to meet the aspirations of candidates who enter the skill
learning domain. As of now, there is no proposal for setting up of new IISs.
10.02.2025 813
The Tata Education and Development Trust (TEDT) has been selected as
private partner in respect of IIS-Mumbai and IIS-Ahmedabad through a competitive
process. No bid was received for IIS-Kanpur. TEDT has set up a non-profit
company i.e. Tata Indian Institute of Skills (TIIS) for the operation and management
of IIS-Mumbai and IIS-Ahmedabad. Land for setting up of IIS at Mumbai and
Ahmedabad was made available by the Government of India and Government of
Gujarat, respectively and the running expenditure is met by Tata IIS. The first
phase of IIS at Mumbai and Ahmedabad has been completed. There is no proposal
under consideration for setting up of more IISs.

SAURASHTRA LANGUAGE IN CENTRAL UNIVERSITIES
1109. DR. GANAPATHY RAJKUMAR P. :
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any proposal to set up Centre for Excellence
for Saurashtra language in any Central University;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
10.02.2025 815
(c) whether the Government has taken any steps to develop linguistic minority
languages in the centre; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN
REGION (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
(a) to (d) There is no proposal to set up Centre of Excellence for Saurashtra
language in any central Universities, at present. As per census, Saurashtra/
Saurashtri is a mother tongue grouped under the language name Gujarati. The
policy of the Government of India is to promote all languages including Saurashtra
language. The National Education Policy, 2020 mandates promotion of
multilingualism and making efforts to keep Indian languages vibrant. NEP focuses
on the promotion of all scheduled and non-scheduled Indian Languages and
provides, wherever possible, for medium of instruction to be in the home
language/mother tongue/local language up to at least class 5 and preferably up to
class 8. The policy also provides for making available high quality text books in
home language/mother tongue and encouraging teachers to use bilingual approach
while teaching. In pursuance of recommendations of NEP, various stakeholders
including University Grants Commission (UGC), All Indian Council for Technical
Education (AICTE), National Council for Educational Research and Training
(NCERT), National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE) have been instructed,
for creation of literature and education materials in different languages for medium
of instructions in mother tongue/Indian Languages. Central Institute of Indian
10.02.2025 816
Languages (CIIL), Mysuru which works for all Scheduled/ Non-Scheduled
languages uploaded Tamil-Saurashtri materials in its Bharatavani portal.
SKILLS ON WHEEL TO RURAL AREAS IN JHARKHAND
1110. DR. NISHIKANT DUBEY:
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any existing initiatives targeted at understanding
and addressing the specific aspirations of rural youth in Jharkhand, if so, the details
thereof;
(b) whether the Government has any plans for up-skilling and providing
employment opportunities to the rural youth who preferred to remain in their
villages, if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government proposes to expand existing initiatives like “Skills on
Wheel” to rural areas in Jharkhand to provide accessible up-skilling opportunities
closer to their homes;
(d) if so, the details thereof;
(e) the details of the enrollment rates in vocational training programmes among
rural youth; and
(f) the steps being taken by the Government to improve awareness and
accessibility of these programmes in Jharkhand?
10.02.2025 817
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b): Under the Govt. of India’s Skill India Mission (SIM), the Ministry of Skill
Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) delivers skill, re-skill and up-skill
training through an extensive network of skill development centres under various
schemes, viz. Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), Jan Sikhshan
Sansthan (JSS), National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) and
Craftsman Training Scheme (CTS) through Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), to
all the sections of the society across the country including rural youth of Jharkhand.
The SIM aims at enabling youth of India to get future ready and industry ready skills.
The brief of these schemes is as under:
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY): PMKVY Scheme is for
imparting skill development training through Short-Term Training (STT) and up-
skilling and re-skilling through Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) to youth across
the country.
Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) Scheme: The main target of the JSS is to impart
vocational skills to the non-literates, neo-literates and the persons having
rudimentary level of education and school dropouts upto 12th standard in the age
group of 15-45 years, with due age relaxation in case of “Divyangjan” and other
deserving cases. Priority is given to Women, SC, ST, OBC and Minorities in the
rural areas and urban low-income areas.
10.02.2025 818
National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS): This Scheme is for
promoting apprenticeship training and increasing the engagement of apprentices
by providing financial support to industrial establishments undertaking
apprenticeship programme under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Training consists of
Basic Training and On-the-Job Training/ Practical Training at workplace in the
industry.
Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS): This scheme is for providing long-term
training through Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) across the country. The ITIs
offer a range of vocational/skill training courses covering a large number of
economic sectors with an objective to provide skilled workforce to the industry as
well as self employment of youth.
(c) and (d): With the objective of improving livelihood of rural households by
providing relevant skills training to its youth population, National Skill Development
Corporation (NSDC), under the aegis of MSDE, is running the 'Skill on Wheels'
program in Ranchi, Jharkhand. This initiative is aimed at ensuring that individuals
in remote areas have access to skilling for improving their employability.
(e) and (f): MSDE has undertaken various strategies to ensure the widespread
reach of programs for skill development across diverse geographies and socio-
economic backgrounds through awareness drives, workshops, and community
engagement initiative. Amplifying awareness around the skilling initiatives is also
undertaken through platforms like All India Radio (AIR), Press Information Bureau
(PIB), MyGov, and Doordarshan (DD). Leveraging digital platforms and mobile
technology, Ministry has ensured accessibility to skill-related information and
10.02.2025 819
resources, benefiting individuals in remote regions. Skill India Digital Hub (SIDH)
serves as a one-stop online resource for youth seeking information and enrollment
in skill training programmes. Ease of access through this platform encourages
participation.
The details of candidates trained under schemes of MSDE during 2023-24
are as under:
PMKVY JSS NAPS* CTS (ITIs)
All India 5,39,325 5,07,337 9,32,298 14,46,247
Jharkhand 8,796 22,439 11,882 40,103
*Apprentice Engaged
SANCTIONED POST AND VACANCIES IN HEIs
1111. SHRI K. RADHAKRISHNAN:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the details of sanctioned posts and vacancies of teachers in various Central
Higher Educational Institutions (HEIs) as on date, institution-wise;
(b) the details of vacancies occurred in Central HEIs during the last two years due
to retirement and the details of vacancies filled up during 2024 and 2025 till date;
and
(c) the number of contractual and ad hoc teachers in these HEIs as on date, HEI-
wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN
REGION (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
10.02.2025 820
(a) to (c) The Central Higher Education Institutions (CHEIs) under Ministry of
Education are statutory autonomous organizations established under respective
Central Acts of Parliament and governed by provisions of the
Acts/Statutes/Ordinances/Regulations made thereunder. As autonomous
institutions, faculty recruitment is done within the institutions itself, in accordance
with their Acts and Regulations. The recruitment powers vests with the respective
Board of Governors/Executive Committee/Board of Management and no active role
of the Ministry is involved therein.
Occurring of vacancies and filling thereof is a continuous process. The
vacancies arise due to promotion, retirement, resignation, death, opening of new
institutions, schemes or projects, and additional requirements on account of
enhanced students’ strength and expansion of capacity in existing institutions.
Ministry of Education had exhorted all the CHEIs to fill up the vacancies in
Mission Mode. From September 2022, all CHEIs have undertaken Mission Mode
recruitment drive to fill the vacancies. Up to 23.12.2024, a total number of 26,751
posts have been filled up by all CHEIs, including 15,637 faculty positions, in Mission
Mode.
The National Education Policy 2020 seeks to transform higher education by
focusing on skill-based education to meet the needs of the industry and the
economy. Further, the NEP also recommends integrating vocational education with
general education and strengthening industry-academia collaboration in CHEIs.
Towards this end, the University Grants Commission (UGC) has taken a new
initiative to bring the industry and other professional expertise into the academic
10.02.2025 821
institutions through “Professor of Practice”. UGC has framed guidelines for
engaging “Professor of Practice” in Universities and Colleges on 30.09.2022. In
order to bring in distinguished experts from various fields of engineering, science,
technology, entrepreneurship etc. and to develop courses and curriculum to meet
the industry and societal needs and to enable the CHEIs to work with industry
experts on joint research project, concept of “Professor of Practice” has been
adopted, and thereby exposure and mentorship to students by domain experts is
provided. Also, visiting faculties are deployed by institutes, as and when required,
so as to avail the benefit of experienced faculty and domain experts.
DEVELOPMENT OF HOLY SHRINE SHABARIKOLLA
1112. SHRI JAGADISH SHETTAR:

(a) whether measures are being contemplated to develop Holy Shrine

and
(b) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) and (b): The development of tourism destinations and products are primarily
undertaken by the concerned State Government (SG)/Union Territory
Administration (UT).
10.02.2025 822
Receiving proposals from the State Governments/UT
Administrations/Central Agencies for financial assistance for tourism projects is a
continuous process. The proposals received are examined with reference to the
prescribed uidelines and financial assistance is extended for such projects subject
to fulfilment of the stipulated conditions and availability of funds.
No proposal is under the consideration of the Ministry to develop Holy Shrine
Shabarikolla in Ramdurg Taluka of Belagavi district in Karnataka as a tourist centre.
However, the Ministry has sanctioned a project in Belagavi district namely
‘Development of Savadatti Yallammagudda in Belgavi” under Special Assistance
to States for Capital Investment (SASCI Scheme) and has also identified a project
in Belagavi District namely “Sri Renuka Yallamma Temple, Saudatti, Belagavi
District” under “Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage Augmentation
Drive” (PRASHAD) Scheme.

िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत (cid:255)ि(cid:248)या और इस संबंध म(cid:164) जारी िदशा-िनद(cid:165)श(cid:332) के अनुसार दी जाती है। पया(cid:170)वरण मंजूरी (ईसी)

से पूण(cid:170) (cid:255)(cid:214) ताव(cid:332) पर अनमु ोदन के िलए िवचार िकया जाता है।

10.02.2025 832
CONSERVATION AND PROMOTION OF NASHIK’S CULTURAL AND
ARCHAEOLOGICAL HERITAGE
1116. SHRI RAJABHAU PARAG PRAKASH WAJE:
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government recognizes Nashik’s immense archaeological and
cultural significance, with its ancient temples, forts, heritage sites and the Godavari
river and if so, the steps taken by the Government for their conservation, restoration
and repairs;
(b) whether the Government acknowledges the importance of preserving Nashik’s
rich cultural heritage, particularly in light of the upcoming Kumbh Mela 2027 and if
so, whether substantial funds will be allocated for this purpose;
(c) if so, the details of ongoing and proposed projects for the conservation of
Nashik’s cultural and archaeological sites, along with timelines for their completion;
(d) whether the Government proposes to establish a regional centre of the
Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in Nashik, given its historical and cultural
importance, if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the Government proposes to provide additional support and resources
to local bodies and stakeholders for effective conservation efforts, ensuring
community involvement and expert guidance, if so, the detail thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) takes all necessary steps towards
conservation and maintenance work, including provision of amenities, at 11
10.02.2025 833
protected monuments/sites in Nashik District of Maharashtra as per the
requirement and availability of resources.
(b) Conservation and preservation of the protected monument is a continuous
process. Adequate funds are allotted as per the requirements of the protected
monuments and approved Annual Conservation Programme (ACP) for the upkeep
of the protected monuments.
(c) The details of conservation, and maintenance work carried out at centrally
protected monuments of Nashik District are given in the enclosed Statement.
(d) No such proposal is under consideration of Government.
(e) ASI undertakes conservation and developmental works on the Monuments,
which in turn generate opportunities of employment for local manpower as well as
skilled craftsmen/artisans, thus ensuring community involvement. Also, as per
requirement, ASI takes the assistance of organisations of repute such as IITs/CBRI
(Central Building Research Institute) for specific studies.

SPORTS ACADEMY AND TRAINING CENTRE IN MURSHIDABAD
1117. SHRI ABU TAHER KHAN:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
10.02.2025 836
(a) whether the Government has allocated funds for the development of sports
infrastructure training and coaching facilities for youth in West Bengal particularly
in Murshidabad Parliamentary Constituency;
(b) if so, the details thereof, year-wise; and
(c) the steps taken or proposed to be taken by the Government to promote rural
youth in sports activity to set up National Sports Academy in Murshidabad
Parliamentary Constituency in West Bengal?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH
AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
(a) to (b) ‘Sports’ being a State subject, the responsibility of development of Sports
infrastructure, training and coaching facilities rests primarily with the State/Union
Territory Governments. Central Government only supplements their efforts by
bridging critical gaps. Funds are allocated scheme-wise and not State-wise in this
Ministry.
Details of funds allocated under the sports development
schemes/programmes of this Department during the last three years are at
Statement.
(c) No such proposal is under consideration for setting up of National Sports
Academy in Murshidabad Parliamentary Constituency in West Bengal. However, 2
National Centre of Excellence (NCoEs), 4 SAI Training Centres (STCs) and 2
Extension Centres are operational in the State of West Bengal for imparting training
to athletes. A total of 398 athletes (boys and girls) are being trained in above
centres. Further, under the Khelo India Scheme, 13 Khelo India Centres (KICs) and
10.02.2025 837
10 Khelo India Accredited Academies (AAs) notified in the State of West Bengal. A
total of 112 athletes (boys and girls) are being trained in above centres.

PM SHRI SCHOOLS IN KARNATAKA
1121. SHRI G. KUMAR NAIK:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the details of schools selected under the PM Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI)
Scheme in Karnataka, district-wise;
(b) the mechanism in place to monitor the implementation and progress of the
scheme, particularly in improving infrastructure and learning outcomes;
(c) the measurable outcomes achieved so far under the scheme, including its
impact on reducing school dropout rate and improving foundational literacy and
numeracy skills; and
(d) whether any mid-term review or impact assessments has been conducted by
the Government in this regard, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a): A total of 555 PM SHRI schools have been selected in the State of
Karnataka. The district-wise number of PM SHRI schools in the State of Karnataka
10.02.2025 845
are given in the enclosed Statement. The details of 555 PM SHRI schools may be
accessed on the website of PM SHRI Scheme at the link:
https://pmshri.education.gov.in/state.
(b): Under the PM SHRI scheme, there is a provision of PRABANDH
(Project Appraisal, Budgeting, Achievements and Data Handling System) portal
and SQAF (School Quality Assessment Framework) to monitor the implementation
and progress of the scheme, including the improvement in infrastructure and
learning outcomes.
The PRABANDH portal of the PM SHRI Scheme has a provision for updating
progress of infrastructure from time to time by States/UTs/KVS/NVS. Further,
SQAF has been developed as per recommendations of National Education Policy
(NEP), 2020, indicating the level of proficiency to be achieved by each PM SHRI
school. SQAF is divided into six main domains, based on the six pillars of the PM
SHRI Scheme, aligned with NEP 2020: Curriculum, Pedagogy, and Assessment;
Access and Infrastructure; Human Resources and School Leadership; Inclusive
Practices and Gender Equity; Management, Monitoring, and Governance and
Beneficiary Satisfaction.
(c): The PM SHRI scheme includes several initiatives to reduce dropout rates and
improve foundational literacy and numeracy. PM SHRI schools create a joyful
learning environment by integrating art, sports, and toy-based pedagogies. There
are provisions for Teaching-learning materials (TLM), including toys, puzzles,
storybooks, and art and craft resources, BaLA features, child-friendly furniture, and
outdoor play materials to create engaging learning spaces. There are provisions for
10.02.2025 846
strengthening educational infrastructure through establishing Atal tinkering labs,
science labs, ICT labs, digital libraries, vocational labs, and sports facilities, etc.
ensuring safe and stimulating learning environment for students. It also has a
provision for child tracking mechanisms to monitor attendance and reduce dropout
rates.
(d): A School Quality Assessment Framework (SQAF) has been developed as
per recommendations of National Education Policy (NEP), 2020, indicating the level
of proficiency to be achieved by each PM SHRI school. SQAF is divided into six
main domains, based on the six pillars of the PM SHRI Scheme, aligned with NEP
2020. Regular monitoring of the same is done by States/UTs and the Centre.

ENFORCEMENT OF WILDLIFE PROTECTION LAW
1122. DR. KALYAN VAIJINATHRAO KALE:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any plan to strengthen existing wildlife protection
law by alleging the Wildlife Protection Act Schedule with the IUCN Red Data Book
to ensure better production of threatened species;
(b) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
10.02.2025 848
(c) whether the Government has taken any measures to improve training and
coordination among various enforcement agencies such as custom police, border
security force and coast guard in detecting and prosecuting wildlife related crimes;
and
(d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) and (b) The Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 has been amended in 2022. This
amendment also included rationalization of Schedules appended to the Act, based
on inputs from Scientific Institutes like Zoological Survey of India, Wildlife Institute
of India, etc.
(c) and (d) The Central Government has constituted the Wild Life Crime Control
Bureau (WCCB) in accordance with section 38Y of the Wild Life (Protection) Act,
1972. The WCCB is mandated to co-ordinate with various officers, State
Governments and other authorities in connection with the enforcement of the
provisions of the Act and also conduct capacity building for scientific and
professional investigation into wildlife crimes.
The WCCB regularly conducts training/capacity building programmes and
meetings with different law enforcement agencies and other stakeholders involved
in protection of wildlife and combating illegal wildlife trade.
10.02.2025 849
INTERNATIONAL CO-OPERATION IN TOURISM SECTOR
1123. DR. THOL THIRUMAAVALAVAN:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any programme for the International Co-
operation in the field of Tourism as it is one of the subject of the Ministry;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government has any plan to promote Tourism in the country
through International Co-operation; and
(d) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) to (d): The Government of India works with other countries to promote
tourism through bilateral and multilateral arrangements that encourage information
sharing, ease of travel, and other areas of cooperation for the promotion and
development of tourism. India is also a member of global tourism organizations like
the United Nations World Tourism Organization (UNWTO), Association of
Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN), Group of Twenty (G20), Group of Seven (G7),
South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC), BRICS (Brazil,
Russia, India, China, and South Africa), etc. helping shape tourism policies and
support sustainable travel. These efforts are to attract more tourists to India and
also to strengthen cultural and economic ties with other nations, contributing to
India's vision of sustainable and inclusive tourism initiatives.
10.02.2025 850
India is promoted as a holistic tourism destination by showcasing its diverse
offerings, including heritage, culture, spirituality, wellness, adventure and eco-
tourism. To promote tourism, the Ministry undertakes several initiatives in
international and domestic markets, including media campaigns, social media
promotions, webinars and participation in promotional events. Additionally, Indian
Missions abroad conduct various activities to attract global travellers to India's
diverse tourist destinations, contribute to strengthening India’s position as a
preferred travel destination on the global map.

ALLOCATION OF GRANTS TO AUTONOMOUS BODIES
1126. DR. MALLU RAVI
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) the details of grants allocated to various autonomous bodies functioning under
the Ministry of Culture during the last three financial years, body-wise and year-
wise;
(b) whether there has been any excess demand for grants by these autonomous
bodies during the said period;
(c) if so, the details thereof, including the reasons for such excess demands and
whether these demands were met; and
(d) if the demands were not met, the details of the reasons for not providing the
additional grants and the measures being taken to ensure sufficient funding for
these bodies in the future?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a): Details of Grants allocated to various Autonomous Bodies under the
administrative control of the Ministry of Culture are given in the enclosed Statement
-I.
(b) and (c): Yes, There has been the demands of excess budget during the said
period by some Autonomous Bodies and the same have been met. Autonomous
Bodies are continuously advised to enhance their internal revenue generation to
meet the excess demand. Details of the excess demand and the reasons therefore
are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
10.02.2025 855
(d): Grants are allocated to ABs keeping in view the overall allocation of the Ministry
and their proposed programmes/activities during the year. Additional grants sought
by the ABs are met through the Supplementary demands as per the GFR
provisions. However, Autonomous Bodies are continuously encouraged to
enhance their internal revenue generation to meet the excess demand.

SETTING UP OF NIIF
1127. SHRI RAVINDRA SHUKLA ALIAS RAVI KISHAN:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) the main objectives of setting up of National Investment and Infrastructure
Fund (NIIF);
(b) the critical sectors in which the NIIF is working; and
(c) the progress made/achievement of NIIF during the last three years?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE
(SHRI PANKAJ CHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b): The objective of National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF)
is to maximise economic impact mainly through infrastructure development in
commercially viable projects, both greenfield and brownfield, including stalled
projects. It could also consider nationally important subjects other nationally
important projects, for example, in manufacturing, if commercially viable. In line with
its stated vision NIIF works on all sectors of national importance including ports and
logistics, renewable energy, roads, airports, smart meters, digital infrastructure,
climate, affordable housing, healthcare, and venture capital/technologies.
(b) The progress made/achievement of the NIIF are given in the enclosed
Statement.
10.02.2025 870
STATEMENT

INCLUSION OF BUXAR IN PRASHAD SCHEME
1128. SHRI SUDHAKAR SINGH:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
10.02.2025 871
(a) the current status of the PRASHAD (Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual
Heritage Augmentation Drive) scheme and the number of projects that have been
implemented under this scheme across the country;
(b) whether the Government has considered inclusion of Buxar, Bihar, under the
PRASHAD scheme, given its historical and spiritual significance as the land where
Lord Ram received his education and the site of the famous Panchkosi Parikrama
Mela, if so, the details thereof;
(c) the details of the specific steps being taken by the Government to develop
and promote tourism in Buxar, including infrastructure development, heritage
preservation and marketing of its cultural significance; and
(d) whether the Government has any plan to collaborate with the Bihar
Government to enhance pilgrimage and cultural tourism in Buxar and other
historically significant sites in the State and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a): The Ministry of Tourism under the “Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual,
Heritage Augmentation Drive” (PRASHAD) Scheme provides financial assistance
to the State Governments and Union Territory Administrations for development of
tourism infrastructure at identified pilgrimage and heritage destinations.
The Ministry of Tourism has sanctioned 47 projects in 27 States/UTs with
estimated cost of Rs.1594.40 crore till date. Details of the projects
sanctioned under the PRASHAD Scheme including their status are given in the
enclosed Statement.
10.02.2025 872
(b) to (d): No proposal is under consideration of the Ministry of Tourism for
inclusion of Buxar, Bihar under the PRASHAD Scheme. However, the Ministry of
Tourism has sanctioned two projects in Bihar under the said scheme namely,
‘Development at Patna Sahib’ and ‘Development of basic facilities at Vishnupad
temple’. In addition, the Ministry of Tourism has identified two sites for development
under the PRASHAD Scheme viz. Simariya Ghat, Begusarai District and Aami
Mandir, Saran District in Bihar.
The Ministry of Tourism as part of its ongoing endeavour also promotes
various tourism destinations and products of the country including Buxar in Bihar
through promotional activities, events, website, social media promotions etc.
Receiving proposals from the State Governments/UT Administrations for
financial assistance for tourism projects is a continuous process. The proposals
received are examined with reference to the prescribed guidelines and financial
assistance is extended for such projects subject to fulfilment of the stipulated
conditions and availability of funds.

(एबीआरवाई) शु(cid:322) क(cid:236) गई थी। लाभािथ(cid:170)य(cid:332) के पंजीकरण क(cid:236) अंितम ितिथ 31.03.2022 थी। 31 माच(cid:170),

(cid:255)दान िकया गया है, िजनम(cid:164) से 4.34 लाख लाभाथ(cid:234) उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश रा(cid:186)य से थे।

1130. डॉ. राजेश िम(cid:174)ा:
(cid:179)या कौशल िवकास और उ(cid:299)मशीलता म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:

ECO-SENSITIVE AREAS IN THE COUNTRY
1131. DR. HEMANT VISHNU SAVARA:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be
pleased to state:
(a) whether it is a fact that to provide special protection to biodiversity, wildlife
and to regulate the developmental activities, Government is notifying the Eco-
sensitive Areas in the Country;
(b) if so, the details thereof, State-wise including Palghar district in Maharashtra;
(c) whether the Government has notified Dahanu Taluka as an Ecologically
Fragile Area in the Palghar district; and
(d) if so, the details thereof and the details of the suggestion received from the
public and the reaction of the Government thereto?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) and (b) In order to provide special protection to landscape, historical value,
biodiversity, and wildlife, and to regulate the developmental activities and ensure
sustainable development, the Central Government in exercise of the powers
conferred under sub-section (3) of Section 3 of the Environment (Protection) Act
1986 and the rules thereunder, has been notifying the Eco-sensitive Areas (ESA)
10.02.2025 880
from time to time. So far, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
(MEFandCC) has notified six ESAs in the country namely Doon Valley and
Bhagirathi in Uttarakhand; Mount-Abu in Rajasthan; and Dahanu Taluka,
Mahabaleshwar-Panchgani and Matheran in Maharashtra.
(c) and (d) A draft notification regarding the Central Government’s intention to
declare Dahanu Taluka, District Thane, Maharashtra as an eco fragile area was
published on 08.02.1991, followed by a corrigendum published on 27.02.1991,
inviting objections from concerned stakeholders. Suggestions / objections received
from various Environmental Action Groups of Dahanu and Bombay, individuals of
Dahanu, Dahanu Industries Association and Dahanu Taluka Krushak Samaj etc on
the draft notification were duly considered while finalization and final publication of
the notification declaring Dahanu Taluka, in Palghar District, Maharashtra as an
ecologically fragile area vide S.O 416 (E), dated the 20th June, 1991 are given at
https://egazette.gov.in/(S(xisl0amuzg0hzrghli5mi24c))/ViewPDF.aspx . The final
notification dated 20.06.1991 imposes restrictions on the setting up of industries
which have detrimental effect on the environment and provides for guidelines
related to location for siting of industries and industrial units. The notification dated
20th June 1991 was amended vide notification dated 22nd February 2021
empowering the State Government to approve master/regional plan for Dhanu
Taluka are given at
https://egazette.gov.in/(S(hrfztiijv2phdwmma0bn1np3))/ViewPDF.aspx The
.
Central Government has followed due procedures as prescribed under the
10.02.2025 881
Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, while issuing the afore-mentioned
notifications.

CENTRAL TRIBAL UNIVERSITY OF ANDHRA PRADESH (CTUAP)
1134. SHRI APPALANAIDU KALISETTI:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the establishment of the Central Tribal University of Andhra Pradesh
(CTUAP) was approved by the Central Government in 2018 and if so, the details
thereof;
(b) whether the site for the CTUAP was changed during the last five years and
if so, the details thereof and the reasons therefor;
(c) the achievements made in terms of infrastructural development, faculty
recruitment and student enrollment during the last five years, year-wise;
(d) the status of academic and administrative operations including the number
of courses offered and students enrolled in the last five years;
(e) the detailed faculty recruitment status, categorized by permanent,
contractual, and vacant positions and steps taken to fill the vacancies; and
(f) the expected timeline for the complete operation and achieving the intended
objectives of the University?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN
REGION (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
10.02.2025 888
(a) to (f) : Central Tribal University of Andhra Pradesh (CTUAP) has been
established in the State of Andhra Pradesh by The Central Universities
(Amendment) Act, 2019 for providing avenues of higher education and research
facilities primarily for the tribal population of India. The State Government of Andhra
Pradesh has provided 561.66 acres of land at Mentada and Dattirajeru Mandal of
District Vizianagaram for establishment of CTUAP. The Government of India has
sanctioned Rs.420 Crore for the establishment of the University out of which an
amount of Rs.124.07 Cr has been released so far.
The University has started functioning in the transit campus at Vizianagaram
District, Andhra Pradesh since academic year 2019-20. Further, 18 faculty posts
were sanctioned which have been filled up by the University. 444 Number of
Students enrolled in 14 courses of the University during the last five years.

िनयिमत उ(cid:198)नयन, एआई, वीएलएसआई, डाटा साइंस आिद जैसे उभरते (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) नए पाठ्य(cid:248)म(cid:332) का

INITIATIVES TO RISE LITERACY RATES IN RURAL AREAS
1136. DR. GUMMA THANUJA RANI:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the current literacy rate across the rural areas of the country,
State-wise;
10.02.2025 890
(b) the steps being taken by the Government to improve literacy rate in these areas
specifically for women and other vulnerable communities;
(c) whether any specific initiatives have been taken by the Government in this
regard during the last three years; and
(d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) A statement indicating State/UT-wise literacy rate across the rural areas of the
country as per Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), Ministry of Statistics and
Programme implementation is given in the enclosed Statement.
(b) to (d) In order to improve the literacy rate including rural literacy rate among
adults in the country, the Government of India has introduced a Centrally
Sponsored Scheme called Nav Bharat Saksharta Karyakram (NILP), popularly
known as ULLAS: Understanding of Lifelong Learning for All in Society from 1st
April, 2022 upto 2027. The scheme, aligned with NEP 2020, targets adults (aged
15 years and above) who couldn’t go to school. The scheme has five components,
namely: (i) Foundational Literacy and Numeracy, (ii) Critical Life Skills, (iii) Basic
Education, (iv) Vocational Skills, and (v) Continuing Education.
The scheme is implemented in a hybrid mode, allowing States/UTs the
flexibility to adopt offline or online mode focusing on imparting foundational literacy
and numeracy including critical life skills, digital and financial literacy. ULLAS works
with the vision of making Bharat ‘Jan Jan Saakshar’, driven by Kartavyabodh (a
10.02.2025 891
sense of duty), utilising school platforms and community engagement through
volunteerism to achieve full literacy. The scheme focuses on rural areas,
educationally backward areas, women and other vulnerable communities etc.
A dedicated ULLAS Mobile App has been developed for registering learners
and volunteer teachers and also enables teaching learning process by providing
access to primers in 26 languages. With continuous efforts, under ULLAS, more
than 2 Crore learners have been registered and more than 1 Crore learners have
already appeared in the Literacy Test named Foundational Literacy and Numeracy
Assessment Test (FLNAT) across the country

EDUCATION/TRAINING PROGRAMME TO EMPOWER WOMEN
1139. SHRI VE. VAITHILINGAM:
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:
10.02.2025 895
(a) whether the Government concurs with the view that improved access to
education and training programmes would empower women with the skills and
knowledge necessary to compete in traditionally male-dominated industries;
(b) if so, the details of the initiatives proposed to be taken by the Government in
this regard; and
(c) if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (c): The Government of India is taking proactive steps to improve
employability of the youth of the nation, including women, through skill
development. Under the Skill India Mission (SIM), the Ministry of Skill Development
and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) delivers skill, re-skill and up-skill training through an
extensive network of skill development centres under various schemes, viz.
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), Jan Sikhshan Sansthan (JSS),
National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) and Craftsman Training
Scheme (CTS) through Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs), to all the sections of the
society including women, across the country. The SIM aims at enabling youth of
India to get future ready and equipped with industry relevant skills.
To encourage participation of women in skill development programs, special
provisions have been made for meeting expenditure on conveyance and boarding
and lodging as well as enhanced post placement support. Further, PMKVY 4.0
prioritizes and provides special focus to projects that emphasize women as primary
10.02.2025 896
beneficiaries. Training programs in sectors like Electronics, Retail, Healthcare,
Beauty and Wellness, Handicrafts and Apparel are structured to attract higher
participation from women. Skill Hubs and Special Projects actively encourage
women's enrolment. Projects are designed to align with local skill demands,
creating opportunities for rural women to participate and benefit from skill
development schemes. This inclusive approach ensures significant representation
and benefit for women in skill training programs across the country. Under JSS
Scheme, the focus is being given to women and other vulnerable sections. Women
constitute more than 80% of beneficiaries under JSS. Also, there are 19 National
Skill Training Institutes (NSTI) and more than 300 ITIs exclusively for
women. Government of India approved 30% reservation of seats for women
candidates in all ITIs (Govt. and Private) in all courses and these seats could be
filled based on the general reservation policy of each respective State/UTs.
STEPS TO ENHANCE BONUS FOR LIC POLICYHOLDERS
1140. SHRI PARSHOTTAMBHAI RUPALA:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has evaluated the workload of individual
employees and branches within LIC and if so, the details thereof and if not, the
reasons therefor;
(b) the steps taken to enhance the vigilance system in LIC and ensure
continuous monitoring to avert any possible manipulation;
10.02.2025 897
(c) the reasons for not mentioning in LIC’s policy documents that every policy
issued by LIC is supported by the Government’s sovereign guarantee in
accordance with section 37 of the LIC Act, 1956;
(d) the measures taken by the LIC to reduce unproductive costs and the reasons
for continuous oversight of these expenses at the senior management level; and
(e) the initiatives taken by the LIC to enhance bonus for all policyholders?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE (SHRI PANKAJ
CHAUDHARY):
(a) LIC is a Board governed commercial entity operating in a competitive and
regulated environment and has a robust performance appraisal system for all
employees. Further performances of individual employee are assessed by LIC
through Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set at the start of financial year and
reviewed twice in a year. Performance of LIC is reviewed at regular intervals by the
Government.
(b) LIC adheres to CVC guidelines to ensure continuous monitoring to avert any
possible manipulation. LIC inter-alia conducts surprise checks, Audits, Inspections,
scrutiny of returns on immovable / movable assets, review of procurement of
materials and service contracts etc. Committees like preventive vigilance
committee and fraud monitoring committee are already in place.
(c) LIC is governed under the provisions of Life Insurance Corporation Act, 1956,
Insurance Act, 1938, IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of
India) Act, 1999 and the rules and regulations made thereunder. Whereas, LIC’s
policy document is a contract between Insurer and Policyholder for the entire
10.02.2025 898
duration / term of the policy. However, the sum assured by all policies including any
bonuses declared in respect thereof is supported by the Government’s sovereign
guarantee as indicated in section 37 of LIC Act.
(d) Budget Review is being done regularly and Expenses of Management (EOM)
are being monitored as per the IRDAI EOM Regulations. Further LIC has taken
measures viz. installing of solar panels, LEDs, sensor operative switches and
conducting most of the meetings and trainings through video conferencing for
reducing expenses.
(e) The bonus payable depends on the surplus generated as result of an annual
actuarial investigation in accordance with relevant rules and regulations. LIC strives
to generate optimal returns by ensuring investment in high quality assets while
ensuring a risk reward balance, which is also reviewed by the Investment
Committee of LIC.
KVs IN UNDERSERVED DISTRICTS
1141. SHRI BENNY BEHANAN:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware that many districts in the country do not
have Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs), if so, the steps being taken by the Government in
this regard;
(b) whether the Government has any plans to establish more KVs in
underserved districts across the country, if so, the details of the proposed locations;
and
10.02.2025 899
(c) the details of the KVs that are currently under consideration for approval, and
the timeline by which these are to be established?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (c) Opening of new Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs) is a continuous process. KVs
are opened primarily to cater to the educational needs of the wards of transferable
Central Government Employees including Defence and Para-military personnel,
Central Autonomous Bodies, Central Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) and
Central Institute of Higher Learning (IHL) by providing a common programme of
education throughout the country. Proposals for opening of new KVs may be
sponsored by Ministries or Departments of the Government of India / State
Governments / Union Territories (UTs) Administrations, with commitment to provide
requisite resources including land and temporary accommodation for setting up a
new KV as per norms. The proposals are subject to approval of the competent
authority as per extant procedures. KVs are not opened on the criteria of district.
At present, there are 1253 KVs functional across the country. The
Government of India has recently approved the proposal for opening of 85 new KVs
under Civil/Defence Sector across the country and expansion of one existing KV
i.e. KV Shivamogga, District Shivamogga, Karnataka by adding 2 additional
Sections in all the classes.

(क) (cid:179)या यह सच ह ै िक महामारी के बाद (cid:174)म शि(cid:265) म(cid:164) मिहलाओ ंक(cid:236) भागीदारी म(cid:164) और िगरावट आई
है और यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है और इसके (cid:179)या कारण ह(cid:167);

उठाए ह(cid:167);

धनरािश आवंिटत क(cid:236) गई है और रा(cid:186)य सरकार(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा िवशेष (cid:322)प स े महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) के यवतमाल-वािशम
संसदीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) िकतनी धनरािश (cid:211)यय क(cid:236) गई है?
सू(cid:224)म, लघु और म(cid:197)यम उ(cid:299)म म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा (cid:174)म और रोजगार म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी
(कु मारी शोभा कारा(cid:198) दलाजे):
(क) से (च): आविधक (cid:174)म बल सव(cid:165)(cid:177)ण (पीएलएफएस) रोजगार और बेरोजगारी के आंकड़े एक(cid:253) करने
का अिधका(cid:229)रक डेटा (cid:261)ोत है िजसे सांि(cid:180)यक(cid:236) और काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन म(cid:253)ं ालय (एमओएसपीआई)
(cid:300)ारा वष(cid:170) 2017-18 से आयोिजत िकया जा रहा है। इस सव(cid:165)(cid:177)ण क(cid:236) अविध (cid:255)ित वष(cid:170) जलु ाई से जून तक
होती है।
नवीनतम उपल(cid:202)ध वािष(cid:170)क पीएलएफएस (cid:229)रपोट(cid:170) के अनुसार, सामा(cid:198)य ि(cid:214)थित के आधार पर 15
वष(cid:170) और उससे अिधक आयु क(cid:236) मिहलाओ ंक(cid:236) अनमु ािनत (cid:174)म बल भागीदारी दर (एलएफपीआर)
2017-18 म(cid:164) 23.3% से बढ़कर 2021-22 म (cid:164) 32.8% और 2023-24 म(cid:164) 41.7% हो गई।
10.02.2025 901
इसके अलावा, नवीनतम वािष(cid:170)क पीएलएफएस (cid:229)रपोट(cid:170) म(cid:164) उपल(cid:202)ध आंकड़(cid:332) के अनसु ार, सामा(cid:198)य
ि(cid:214)थित के आधार पर 15 वष(cid:170) और उससे अिधक आयु क(cid:236) मिहलाओ ंक(cid:236) रोजगार को दशा(cid:170)ने वाला देने
वाला अनुमािनत कामगार जनसं(cid:180)या अनुपात (ड(cid:202)(cid:208)यूपीआर) 2017-18 म(cid:164) 22.0% से बढ़कर 2023-
24 म(cid:164) 40.3% हो गया।
िनयोजनीयता म(cid:164) सुधार करते ह(cid:242)ए रोजगार का सृजन करना सरकार क(cid:236) (cid:255)ाथिमकता रही है।
सरकार मिहला (cid:174)म बल भागीदारी दर (एलएफपीआर) को बढ़ावा देने के साथ-साथ सम(cid:250) एलएफपीआर
को बढ़ावा देने के िलए िविभ(cid:198)न योजनाए ं जैसे (cid:255)धानम(cid:253)ं ी म(cid:254)ु ा योजना (पीएमएमवाई), (cid:214)ट(cid:167)ड-अप इंिडया
(cid:214)क(cid:236)म, (cid:214)टाट(cid:170)अप इंिडया, (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी रोजगार सृजन काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (पीएमईजीपी), िव(cid:178)ान और इंजीिनय(cid:229)रंग
म(cid:164) मिहलाए-ं िकरण (ड(cid:202)(cid:208)यूआईएसई-िकरण), एसईआरबी-पावर (अ(cid:198)वेषणा(cid:194)मक अनसु ंधान म(cid:164) मिहलाओ ं
के िलए अवसर(cid:332) को बढ़ावा देना), िमशन शि(cid:265), नमो (cid:369)ोन दीदी और लखपित दीदी, महा(cid:194)मा गांधी रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय
(cid:250)ामीण रोजगार गारंटी योजना (एमजीएनआरईजीएस), पंिडत दीन दयाल उपा(cid:197)याय (cid:250)ामीण कौश(cid:208)य
योजना (डीडीयू-जीके वाई), (cid:250)ामीण (cid:214)वरोजगार और (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण स(cid:214)ं थान (आरएसईटीआई), दीन दयाल
अ(cid:194)ं योदय योजना-रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय शहरी आजीिवका िमशन (डीएवाई-एनयूएलएम), उ(cid:194)पादन संब(cid:294) (cid:255)ो(cid:194)साहन
आिद काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रही है। भारत सरकार (cid:300)ारा काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत क(cid:236) जा रही िविभ(cid:198)न रोजगार सजृ न
योजनाओ/ंकाय(cid:170)(cid:248)म(cid:332) का (cid:202)यौरा https://dge.gov.in/dge/schemes_programmes पर देखा जा
सकता है।
महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) सिहत दशे म(cid:164) िपछले पांच वष(cid:334) म (cid:164) ऐसी योजनाओ ंके लाभािथ(cid:170)य(cid:332) के (cid:202)यौरे और उपयोग
क(cid:236) गई िनिधय(cid:332) के (cid:202)यौर े संल(cid:181)न िववरण- I स े VI म(cid:164) िदए गए ह(cid:167)।
मिहला कामगार(cid:332) क(cid:236) रोजगार (cid:177)मता बढ़ाने के िलए, सरकार मिहला औ(cid:299)ोिगक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण
स(cid:214)ं थान(cid:332), रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय (cid:211)यावसाियक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण सं(cid:214)थान(cid:332) और (cid:177)े(cid:253)ीय (cid:211)यावसाियक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण सं(cid:214)थान(cid:332) के नेटवक(cid:170)
के मा(cid:197)यम स े भी उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण (cid:255)दान कर रही है।
10.02.2025 902
मिहला कामगार(cid:332) के िलए अनुकू ल काय(cid:170) वातावरण हेत ु(cid:174)म कानून(cid:332) म(cid:164) सवैतिनक मातृ(cid:194)व अवकाश,
िशशु देखभाल अवकाश, समान मजदूरी आिद जैसे अनेक (cid:255)ावधान भी शािमल िकए गए ह(cid:167)। इन सभी
पहल(cid:332) से दीघ(cid:170) अविध म(cid:164) गणु क (cid:255)भाव के मा(cid:197)यम से इक(cid:281)े रोजगार सजृ न होने क(cid:236) उ(cid:204)मीद है।
इसके अलावा, (cid:174)म और रोजगार म(cid:253)ं ालय ने जनवरी, 2024 म(cid:164) "मिहला काय(cid:170)बल भागीदारी को
बढ़ावा देने के िलए िनयो(cid:265)ाओ ंके िलए परामिश(cid:170)का" जारी क(cid:236)। इस परामिश(cid:170)का म(cid:164) अ(cid:198)य बात(cid:332) के साथ-
साथ प(cid:321)ु ष(cid:332) और मिहलाओ ंदोन(cid:332) के िलए रोजगार और देखभाल क(cid:236) िज(cid:204)मेदा(cid:229)रय(cid:332) के बीच संतुलन क(cid:236)
आव(cid:212)यकता का उ(cid:208)लेख िकया गया है, िजसम(cid:164) िपतृ(cid:194)व अवकाश, माता-िपता अवकाश, पा(cid:229)रवा(cid:229)रक
आपातकालीन छु(cid:280)ी और लचीली कामकाजी (cid:211)यव(cid:214)था जैसे प(cid:229)रवार अनकु ू लन उपाय शािमल ह(cid:167)।
इसके अलावा, क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय बजट (2024-25) म(cid:164) काय(cid:170)बल म(cid:164) मिहलाओ ंक(cid:236) भागीदारी बढ़ाने के िलए
उ(cid:299)ोग जगत के सहयोग से कामकाजी मिहला हॉ(cid:214)टल(cid:332) और िशशुगहृ क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना क(cid:236) घोषणा क(cid:236) गई।
