10.02.2025 915
WOMEN ENTREPRENEURSHIP IN ODISHA
1143. SHRI ANANTA NAYAK
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Women-Led Programme aims to address the challenges faced
by women in entrepreneurship through crucial skills, knowledge and networking
opportunities, particularly in Odisha;
(b) the details of number of programmes that aims to empower women in
Odisha, including rural and tribal areas such as Keonjhar;
(c) the details of efforts undertaken by the Government to collaborate with
private and public sector entities to empower women entrepreneurs in Odisha; and
(d) the details of specific initiatives or partnerships that have been launched in
the said State?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (d) The Government of India recognizes the importance of offering crucial
skills, knowledge and networking opportunities to women entrepreneurs to address
the challenges faced by them in overcoming societal, economic and structural
barriers, lack of access to business opportunities and training. The Government
has taken various initiatives to build entrepreneurial mindsets and capabilities
among women, support the growth of women-led enterprises, and provide
10.02.2025 916
mentoring and handholding support to address the challenges faced by them in
entrepreneurship.
The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) through its
autonomous organizations, namely National Institute for Entrepreneurship and
Small Business Development (NIESBUD) and Indian Institute of Entrepreneurship
(IIE) has taken various initiatives to promote entrepreneurship development
amongst all sections of the society, including women across the country, including
the state of Odisha. The details of such initiatives undertaken by the Ministry are
given in the enclosed Statement-I.
Further, there are various schemes/ programmes being implemented by
Ministries/ Departments of Government of India with the objective of promoting
entrepreneurs, including female entrepreneurs. The details of some of the key
initiatives implemented by other Ministries/ Departments in the state of Odisha are
given in the enclosed Statement-II.
MSDE has collaborated with both private and public sector to empower
women entrepreneurship by providing incubation support, network of mentors,
industry connect, credit and market linkages. NIESBUD has signed a Memorandum
of Understanding (MoU) with Meta to support the Indian entrepreneurial ecosystem.
The aim of the MoU is to provide aspiring and current small business owners,
including women, with the necessary tools, knowledge, and resources to thrive in
today's dynamic market environment. The partnership will help in training budding
and existing entrepreneurs in digital marketing skills in seven regional languages.
10.02.2025 917
During Financial Year 2024-25, a total number of 191 participants have been
imparted training through this initiative in Odisha
NIESBUD, in collaboration with Hindustan Unilever Limited (HUL), organized
Entrepreneurship Awareness Programmes (EAPs) with the goal of promoting
entrepreneurship as a viable livelihood option among unemployed youths, school
dropouts, and students, including women. The project is sponsored by HUL under
its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiative. The project aims to encourage
self-employment and foster entrepreneurship across India. During Financial Years
2023-24 and 2024-25, a total of 5272 participants have been trained in Odisha
through the EAPs, of which 3532 are women.
STATEMENT-I
The details of various initiatives being implemented by the Ministry of Skill
Development and Entrepreneurship for women entrepreneurs in Odisha are
as under:
1. Strengthening Entrepreneurial Climate through Capacity Building,
Incubation Support, Mentoring, and Handholding under SANKALP Scheme –
MSDE through its autonomous institute, NIESBUD implemented a project for
strengthening the entrepreneurship ecosystem of different marginalized sections of
the society and women under the Skills Acquisition and Knowledge Awareness for
Livelihood Promotion (SANKALP) Scheme since December 2022. The scheme
aimed at creating, fostering and promoting the spirit of entrepreneurship among the
various target groups through Capacity Building, Incubation Support, Mentoring
and Handholding. SANKALP has been implemented in three phases. Under
SANKALP 1, a total of 23825 beneficiaries have been trained during Financial
10.02.2025 918
Years 2022-23 to 2024-25, out of which 13861 are women, including 2029 women
beneficiaries in Odisha. Under SANKALP 2, a total of 23841 beneficiaries have
been trained during Financial Year 2022-23 to 2024-25, out of which 18270 are
women, including 919 women beneficiaries in Odisha. Under SANKALP 3, a total
of 4274 beneficiaries have been trained during Financial Year 2024-25, out of which
2970 are women, including 315 women beneficiaries in Odisha. In Odisha,
SANKALP is being implemented in the districts of Gajapati, Dhenkanal, Kendujhar
(Keonjhar), Rayagada, Mayurbhanja, Bargarh (Baragarh), Malkangiri, Khordha,
Koraput, Sundergarh, Gajapati, Nabarangpur, Kalahandi, Bubaneswar, Balangir,
Ganjam, Sambalpur, Baleshwar, Kandhamal and Nuapada.
2. Skill Strengthening for Industrial Value Enhancement (STRIVE) - Under the
Skill Strengthening for Industrial Value Enhancement (STRIVE) of MSDE,
NIESBUD has conducted awareness and sensitization programmes at National
Skill Training Institutes (NSTIs) and Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) between
October 2023 to May 2024. These activities were followed by the selection of
prospective entrepreneurs to undergo the Entrepreneurship Development
Programme (EDP). The Institute has successfully conducted EDP training for the
target groups and is providing mentoring and handholding support to help trainees
establish their enterprises. A total of 45339 beneficiaries have been trained under
the project during Financial Years 2023-24 to 2024-25, out of which 12437 are
women, which includes 680 women beneficiaries in Odisha. In Odisha, trainings
under the initiative were conducted in the districts of Bhubaneswar, Cuttack,
Kendujhar (Keonjhar) and Sundergarh.
10.02.2025 919
3. Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN) - MSDE
through its autonomous institute, NIESBUD has been implementing the skilling and
entrepreneurship component of Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha
Abhiyaan (PM JANMAN) – a scheme of Ministry of Tribal Affairs for upliftment of
Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs), since March 2024. The project is
being implemented with support of Tribal Cooperative Marketing Development
Federation of India (TRIFED) in 18 States across the country under which a total
of 500 VDVKs are to be set up. A total of 39576 beneficiaries have been trained
under the project till January 2025, out of which 31732 are women, including 3410
women beneficiaries in Odisha. In Odisha, the initiative is being implemented in the
districts of Angul, Kendujhar (Keonjhar), Gajapati, Kendujhar (Keonjhar),
Malkangiri, Mayurbhanja, Nuapada and Sundergarh.
4. Rashtriya Udyamita Vikas Pariyojana on Pilot Basis for PM SVANidhi
Beneficiaries - MSDE, in collaboration with the Ministry of Housing and Urban
Affairs (MoHUA), launched the Rashtriya Udyamita Vikas Pariyojana (RUVP) on a
pilot basis in February 2024 for PM SVANidhi beneficiaries, with NIESBUD as one
of the implementing partners. Through the project, entrepreneurship training was
provided to 1744 PM-SVANidhi beneficiaries across 10 cities, with more than 40%
participation from women. The program included one week of classroom training
followed by 21 weeks of mentoring and hand-holding support. A total of 1744
beneficiaries have been trained under the project during Financial Year 2023-24,
out of which 1205 are women, including 92 women beneficiaries in Sambalpur
district of Odisha.
10.02.2025 920
5. Entrepreneurial Climate through Training of Trainers (ToTs) and
Entrepreneurship Development Programme (EDP) in Jan Shikshan
Sansthans (JSSs) - NIESBUD has implemented a project for creating
entrepreneurial climate through the Training of Trainers (ToTs) and the
Entrepreneurship Development Programme (EDP) in Jan Shikshan Sansthans
(JSSs) from October 2022 to September 2023. The project aimed at creating,
fostering and promoting the spirit of entrepreneurship among various target groups
through Capacity Building, Mentoring and Handholding. A total of 5739
beneficiaries have been trained under the project during Financial Years 2022-23
to 2023-24, out of which 4771 are women, including 564 women beneficiaries in
Bhadrak, Boudh (Baudh), Cuttack, Debagarh (Deogarh), Dhenkanal, Gajapati,
Ganjam, Jagatsinghapur, Jajpur, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Kendrapara, Khordha,
Koraput, Nuapada, Puri and Bhubaneswar districts of Odisha.
6. Pilot Project for Entrepreneurship Development Program (EDP) - NIESBUD
implemented a Pilot Project for Entrepreneurship Development Program in 10
States supported by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, from
January 2020 to March 2023, which aimed at creating, fostering and promoting the
spirit of entrepreneurship among the target groups through Entrepreneurship
Development Programme, Mentoring and Handholding support. A total of 5000
beneficiaries have been trained under the project during Financial Years 2019-20
to 2022-23, out of which 3089 are women, including 418 women beneficiaries in
Balangir, Baleshwar, Bargarh, Cuttack, Ganjam, Kandhamal and Nabarangpur
districts of Odisha.
10.02.2025 921
7. Digital Marketing and Entrepreneurship Development for Women Self Help
Groups - NIESBUD implemented a project on Digital Marketing and
Entrepreneurship Development for Women Self Help Groups (WSHGs) in Odisha,
supported by Odisha Skill Development Authority from March 2022 to July 2022,
for capacity building and to enable them to access the market through e-commerce
platforms. The project has been piloted in 10 Aspirational districts of Odisha namely
Bolangir, Dhenkanal, Rayagada, Malkangiri, Nabarangpur, Kalahandi, Gajapati,
Kandhamal, Nuapada and Koraput. A total number of 1993 WSHG members have
been imparted training in Odisha under the project during Financial Years 2021-22
to 2022-23.
8. Pilot Project on Entrepreneurship Development in the Holy City - MSDE
implemented a Pilot Project on Entrepreneurship Promotion and Mentoring of Micro
and Small Businesses in six temple towns, namely Bodh Gaya, Haridwar, Kollur,
Pandharpur, Puri, and Varanasi from December 2021 to July 2023. IIE
implemented the project in three Holy Cities i.e. Bodh Gaya, Kollur and Puri. A total
of 1318 women participants have benefited in the Puri district of Odisha through
the project during Financial Years 2021-22 to 2022-23.
STATEMENT-II
The details of various initiatives being implemented by other Ministries/
Departments of Government of India for women entrepreneurs across
country, including the state of Odisha are as under:
1. Ministry of Tribal Affairs (MoTA) - The Ministry of Tribal Affairs through its two
agencies namely Tribal Co-operative Marketing Development Federation of India
(TRIFED) and National Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation
10.02.2025 922
(NSTFDC) has contributed significantly in promoting economic activities being
undertaken by the tribal communities and has impacted in turn their economic
development. NSTFDC is implementing Adivasi Mahila Sashaktikaran Yojana
(AMSY) which is an exclusive scheme for economic development of Scheduled
Tribes Women, including the Scheduled Tribes Women in Odisha. Under the
scheme, NSTFDC provides loan up to 90% for projects costing up to ₹2.00 lakh at
highly concessional rate of interest of 4% per annum.
Livelihood Incubation Centre at Rourkela (Odisha) for Tribal Livelihood is a
collaborative project having participation of three organizations namely National
Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), Rourkela Steel Plant (Steel Authority of
India) and National Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation
(NSTFDC). It is a specific initiative aimed at training and fostering entrepreneurship
within the tribal communities of Odisha.
2. Department of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare is implementing “Innovation
and Agri- Entrepreneurship Development” programme under Rashtriya Krishi Vikas
Yojana (RKVY) with an objective to promote innovation and agri-entrepreneurship
by providing financial support and nurturing an incubation ecosystem in the country.
Five Knowledge Partners (KPs) and twenty-four RKVY Agribusiness Incubators (R-
ABIs) provide training and incubate Startups for implementation of this programme.
Under this programme, for idea/pre-seed stage, a selected Start-up shall be
eligible for maximum financial assistance of Rupees Five Lakh in one installment.
For seed stage, a selected Start-up shall be eligible for maximum financial
assistance of Rupees Twenty-Five Lakh in two installments of 50% and 50% based
10.02.2025 923
on the recommendation given by the Selection and Investment Committee (SIC).
Each KP can select a maximum 20-25 Start-ups and each R-ABI can select a
maximum 10-12 start-ups in each category of pre seed and seed stage in a financial
year.
Trainings, technical and financial assistance is provided to Startups to launch
their products, services, business platforms, etc. into the market and facilitate them
to scale up their products and operations to attain business viability. More than
5000 Agri-Startups have been trained by KPs and R-ABIs under this programme
during FY 2019-20 to 2024-25.
Under the “Innovation and Agri-Entrepreneurship Development” programme
of PM-RKVY during 2019-20 to 2024-25, financial assistance of Rs. 124.96 crore
has been released to 1749 Agri-Startups including 448 women
entrepreneurs/Startups through various KPs and R-ABIs. In the State of Odisha,
62 Startups have been supported under the programme. In Odisha, National Rice
Research Institute, Cuttack has been appointed as R-ABI for the implementation of
the programme.
3. Ministry of Women and Child Development - Government of India implements
'Mission Shakti' – an integrated women empowerment programme as umbrella
scheme for safety, security and empowerment of women. The Umbrella Scheme of
Mission Shakti has two sub-schemes namely "Sambal" and "Samarthya”.
'Samarthya' sub-scheme, has a component i.e. Hub for Empowerment of
Women (HEW) known as SANKALP: HEW (Supportive Action for Nurturing and
Knowledge-Based Advancement, Last-Mile Delivery and Potential Realization of
10.02.2025 924
Women : Hub for Empowerment of Women), set up at National, State and District
levels for empowerment of women with aims to facilitate inter-sectoral convergence
of schemes and programs meant for women at Central, State/ UT and district levels
with the mandate to create an environment in which women realize their full
potential to address gaps in state action for women and for promoting inter-
ministerial and inter-sectoral convergence through strengthening the processes
that promote holistic empowerment of women by creating an environment
conducive to social change by improving access and utilization of government
schemes.
The support under the HEW is for guiding, linking and hand holding women
to various institutional and schematic set ups for their empowerment and
development including facilitating access to healthcare, quality education, career
and vocational counselling/ training, financial inclusion, entrepreneurship,
backward and forward linkages, health and safety for workers, social security and
digital literacy at districts/ Blocks/ Gram Panchayats level across the country.”
SANKALP: HEW is being implemented in all States/UTs except the State of
West Bengal and functional in 35 States/UTs. As on date, total 35 State SANKALP
HEWs and 742 District SANKALP: HEWs are functional.
4. Department of Science and Technology
4.1 National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations (NIDHI) - In
order to foster innovations in the country, the Department of Science and
Technology has been supporting various activities under National Initiative for
Developing and Harnessing Innovations (NIDHI) by providing them training in
10.02.2025 925
entrepreneurship skills, access to incubation facilities, mentorship, and tailored
funding mechanisms. These initiatives empower and encourage innovators and
startups, including the women led startups to convert their ideas into a successful
business model. Financial support is provided through Technology Business
Incubators (TBIs) for Mentorship, incubation support including lab access and
timely availability early-stage funding through NIDHI Entrepreneurs-in-Residence
(EIR) program; NIDHI-Promoting and Accelerating Young and Aspiring Innovators
and Startups (PRAYAS); NIDHI-Accelerator and NIDHI-Seed Support Program
(NIDHI-SSP) to incubated startups. In order to strengthen innovation and startup
incubation ecosystem in Tier II and Tier III cities, a new program named NIDHI-
Inclusive Technology Business Incubators (iTBI) program was launched by DST to
support startup incubation centres in academic setups in tier II and tier III cities.
The iTBI program has helped in increasing entrepreneurial inclusiveness in terms
of geographies, gender and persons with special abilities.
4.2 Women Entrepreneurship Development Programme (WEDP) - Ministry of
Science of Technology implements ‘Innovation based Science and Technology
Entrepreneurship Training Programme’ which focuses on Innovation and
Entrepreneurship (IandE) training for participants and encourages participants to
take entrepreneurship as a career from all over the country including the women
from the rural and tribal area of State of Odisha. Under the scheme, Science and
Technology based Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IandE) training programmes
are implemented by the Institute of higher learning, Incubators, SandT park and
Non-Governmental Organization etc; for the participants including the specific
10.02.2025 926
programme on Women Entrepreneurship Development Programme (WEDP). From
Financial Years 2019-20 to 2022-23, a total of 327 women have been trained under
WEDP in Odisha.
4.3 Science and Technology for Women (STW) programme - STW aims to
improve the weakest link of the predominant livelihood system of women in an area
and promote social entrepreneurship based on the strongest link of the livelihood
system through interventions of Science, Technology and Innovations. The Women
Technology Parks (WTP) act as resource centers under the STW programme and
provide necessary support to women from a single platform for their training and
capacity building in various technologies, for increasing health and nutrition level in
women through development of value-added food, herbal medicines, diet
supplements, etc. Efforts are being made that women in rural and peri-urban areas
receive training on live technology models demonstrated in WTPs for skill
development and capacity building as per the requirement of SandT based
interventions, using area specific resources, with goal of achieving self- reliance
locally for economic re-growth.
5. Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) - The
Government launched the Startup India initiative on 16th January 2016 with an
intent to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation, startups and
encouraging investments in the startup ecosystem of the country. As on 31st
December 2024, 1,57,706 entities have been recognized as startups by DPIIT, out
of which 2769 are from the Odisha. From the recognized startups, a total of 75,935
10.02.2025 927
entities self-reported to have at least one-woman director/ partner, out of which
1386 are woman led startups from the state of Odisha.
Further, the Government is implementing specific initiatives/programmes
under the Startup India initiative to promote women entrepreneurship across the
country. The details of such initiatives are as under:
i.To promote flow of both equity and debt to women led startups, 10% of the fund
in the Fund of Funds for Startups Scheme operated by Small Industries
Development Bank of India (SIDBI) is reserved for women-led startups.
ii.Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) led by women are eligible to be considered
for a higher level of management fee (0.1% per annum). The same benefit is also
extended to AIFs which are focused on women led startups.
iii.Under Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS), for availing the guarantee
cover, the Member Institution pays Annual Guarantee Fee (AGF) of 2% p.a. of the
disbursement/outstanding amount. For units from the North East region as well as
those of women entrepreneurs, the Member Institution pays a standard rate of
1.5% p.a. of the disbursement/outstanding amount.
iv.Women Capacity Development Programme (WING) is a unique Capacity
Development Program for women-led startups, to identify and support both
aspiring and established women entrepreneurs in their startup journeys. The
workshops are open to variety of business sectors including Tech, Construction,
Product, Machine, Food, Agriculture, Education, etc. The workshops serve as a
platform for emerging women entrepreneurs and other stakeholders to discuss
10.02.2025 928
the key challenges facing women entrepreneurs. WING workshops create a
conducive environment to share best practices and experiences in overcoming
challenges and to gain insights learned from the business models adopted in the
Indian context.
v.A Virtual Incubation Program for Women Entrepreneurs was conducted to support
women-led tech startups with pro-bono acceleration support.
vi.Startup India Hub: A webpage dedicated to women entrepreneurs has been
designed on the Startup India portal. The page includes various policy measures
for women entrepreneurs by both Central and State Governments.
vii.ASCEND Startup Workshop Series and Women for Startups Workshops: The
Government organized a series of startup workshops - ASCEND (Accelerating
Startup Calibre and Entrepreneurial Drive), for the entrepreneurs, aspiring
entrepreneurs, and students from North-eastern region. In addition, the
workshops are conducted with a specific focus on women entrepreneurs across
the northeastern states. The workshops have witnessed participation from
ecosystem stakeholders such as government officials, startups, aspiring
entrepreneurs, investors, academic institutions, etc.
viii.SuperStree Podcast: With a vision to inspire a greater number of women across
all regions in India to become entrepreneurs, the SuperStree video podcast series
has been launched on women in the Indian Startup Ecosystem. The podcast
spreads awareness related to innovations from women and to further strengthen
women entrepreneurship in the country.
10.02.2025 929
ix.Through various awareness programmes and capacity building programmes
organized by the Government, and through print media and social media
platforms, the Government also creates awareness about the existing schemes
which support micro, small and medium entrepreneurs, including women
entrepreneurs.
x.Women For Startups: State Workshops for Women Entrepreneurs were held
across States to build the capacity of aspiring and existing women entrepreneurs.
The workshops focused on government scheme awareness, mock pitching, and
finance-related training.
xi.The States’ Startup Ranking on support to startup ecosystems is primarily an
exercise to identify good practices across all the Indian states. The evaluation
includes a specific provision to gauge the formulation and implementation of
policies and special incentives to promote women-led startups in each state. The
particular action point has witnessed active engagement and thereon reporting of
measures undertaken by participating States and UTs.
xii.To identify the depth, quality and spread of innovation, inclusivity and diversity,
and entrepreneurship in country, the Government instituted the National Startup
Awards (NSA). NSA recognizes and promotes startups across 20 sectors and
special categories. All four editions of the NSA (2020, 2021, 2022 and 2023) have
featured a special category and award for women-led startups.
xiii.Pitch Forward is an initiative to provide women entrepreneurs, especially from
non-metros, a platform to connect with marquee investors. It offers startups across
10.02.2025 930
various stages and sectors the opportunity to pitch directly to venture capital
funds.
6. Ministry of Minority Affairs – The Ministry of Minority Affairs implemented “Nai
Roshni”, a Leadership Development programme for Minority Women, which was
launched in 2012-13 with an objective to empower and instill confidence among
minority women by providing knowledge, tools and techniques for interacting with
Government systems, banks and other institutions at all levels. Since inception,
about 6,375 beneficiaries have been trained under the scheme in the State of
Odisha. The training module cover areas related to programmes for Women, Health
and Hygiene, Legal rights of women, Financial Literacy, Digital Literacy, Swachh
Bharat, Life Skills, and Advocacy for Social and Behavioural changes. The scheme
was implemented through Programme Implementing Agencies/ Non-Governmental
Organizations.
Nai Roshni Scheme has now been converged as a component into an
integrated scheme called ‘Pradhan Mantri Virasat ka Samvardhan (PM VIKAS)’,
which has three sub-components namely Leadership and Basic Entrepreneurship
Development, Entrepreneurship Development and Business Mentors/ Business
Correspondents (Biz Sakhis/Udyami Mitra).
7. Ministry of Rural Development - Rural Self Employment Training Institutes
(RSETIs), funded by the Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD) and led by banks,
provide skill and entrepreneurship training to rural youth, including women, to
promote self-employment and reduce poverty. The program targets unemployed
10.02.2025 931
youth aged 18-45 with basic knowledge in relevant fields, equipping them for self-
employment or wage employment. While many graduates pursue
entrepreneurship, some may also seek for wage employment.
MoRD finances the construction of RSETI buildings and covers training costs for
the rural poor. Free skill based residential training programs are being conducted
at 605 RSETIs across the country including 30 Rural Self Employment Training
Institutes (RSETIs) in the state of Odisha. These RSETIs impart training in 64
MoRD and NCVET approved courses, free of cost, to the rural unemployed youth.
Nearly 70 % of the total trainees in RSETIs comprise of rural women. There are
nine exclusive women centric training programmes in RSETIs through which
women can develop skill and entrepreneurship.
Skill development program through Rural Self Employment Training
Institutes (RSETIs) also enables a trainee to take Bank credit and start his/her own
Micro-enterprise. Some of such trainees may also seek regular salaried jobs. The
details of trained and settled candidates in Odisha State for last three financial
years and current financial year as on 31.12.2024 are provided below:

8. Ministry of Food Processing Industry (MoFPI) has launched the Pradhan
Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) scheme
under the Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan with the aim to enhance the
competitiveness of existing individual micro-enterprises in the unorganized
segment of the food processing industry and promote formalization of the sector.
The scheme has a special focus on supporting Groups engaged in Agri-food
processing such as Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Self Help Groups
(SHGs) and Producers Cooperatives along their entire value chain. SHG members
can receive seed capital of ₹40,000 per member for working capital and the
purchase of small tools. The scheme also provides a 50% financial grant for
branding and marketing support to groups of FPOs, SHGs, Cooperatives, or a
Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) of micro food processing enterprises. Under the
PMFME Scheme, 301 SHG beneficiaries got benefitted from seed capital in the
10.02.2025 933
State of Odisha and the National Institute of Food Technology, Entrepreneurship
and Management, Thanjavur (NIFTEM-T), an autonomous institute under MoFPI,
provided hands-on training to 34 women entrepreneurs from Odisha in various
domains.
9. Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (M/o MSME) - The
Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programme (ESDP) Scheme of M/o
MSME is a Central Sector scheme, which was up scaled in November 2019. The
objective of the programme is to motivate young persons representing different
sections of the society including SC/ST women, Persons with Disabilities(PwD), ex-
servicemen, and Below Poverty Line persons to consider self-employment or
entrepreneurship as one of the career options. The ultimate objective is to promote
new enterprises, build capacity of existing MSMEs and inculcate entrepreneurial
culture in the country. New ESDP guideline, which was approved on 25.3.2022,
has five components namely:
i. Entrepreneurship Awareness Programme (EAP)
ii. Entrepreneurship cum Skill Development Programme (ESDP)
iii. Advance ESDP - This component is newly added and is to be conducted by
premier Institutes like IITs/IIMs/ ICAR/BARC/Agricultural Universities of State
/Central Governments.
iv. Management Development Programme (MDP)
v. Advance MDP- It is newly added in the new guidelines approved for the
15th Finance Commission which includes programme to be conducted by
premier institutes like that of Advance ESDP component.
10.02.2025 934
From Financial Years 2022-23 to 2024-25 (till 07.02.2025), a total of 22,273
women beneficiaries have been trained in the State of Odisha, including 574 in
Keonjhar district of Odisha.
10. Ministry of Textiles is implementing Samarth (Scheme for Capacity Building
in Textiles Sector) with the objective to provide demand driven, placement-oriented
skilling programmes to supplement the efforts of the industry in creating jobs in the
organized textile and related sectors, covering the entire value chain of textiles,
excluding Spinning and Weaving in organized sector. Samarth is implemented on
demand driven basis across the country and preference is given to marginalized
social groups such as women, SC/ ST etc. under the scheme. As on 07.02.2025,
3,59,037 beneficiaries have been trained (passed) and of these 3,16,275 are
women.
11. Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment (MoSJandE) implements a
Venture Capital Fund (VCF) Scheme for Scheduled Caste (VCF-SC) and Backward
Classes (VCF-BC). These Schemes have been launched with the objective of
promoting entrepreneurship amongst the said communities through concessional
finance. At present a coupon rate of 4% is charged under VCF-SC and 6% under
VCF-BC. To promote women entrepreneurs, a further rebate of 0.25% percent is
provided. Further, to promote young researchers, including women, “Ambedkar
Social Innovation Incubation Mission (ASIIM)” under VCF-SC is being implemented
since 30th September 2020. These schemes are operational and applicable
throughout the country including the state of Odisha.
10.02.2025 935

 िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) के िलए रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय (cid:211)यावसाियक मानक (एनपीएसटी) िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) के काय(cid:170) को प(cid:229)रभािषत

जो छा(cid:253)(cid:332) के िलए शैि(cid:177)क प(cid:229)रणाम(cid:332) म (cid:164) सधु ार करगे ा। एनपीएसटी माग(cid:170)दश(cid:170)क द(cid:214)तावेज़ िदनांक 9 माच(cid:170)

देश म(cid:164) िश(cid:177)ा क(cid:236) भावी योजना बनाना था। स(cid:302)ाह का (cid:255)(cid:194)येक िदन एक िविश(cid:310) िवषय के िलए समिप(cid:170)त था,

(ग): (cid:214)कू ली िश(cid:177)ा के िलए रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय पाठ्य(cid:248)म (cid:322)परखे ा (एनसीएफएसई), 2023 म(cid:164) सामािजक भाषाई

1. रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय पाठ्यचया(cid:170) (cid:322)परखे ा (एनसीएफ): रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय पाठ्यचया(cid:170) (cid:322)परेखा (एनसीएफ) का िवकास िश(cid:177)ा
(cid:255)णाली को (cid:178)ान-आधा(cid:229)रत (cid:255)णाली क(cid:236) ओर ले जाने और छा(cid:253)(cid:332) के तनाव को कम करने के िलए मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170)
है। रटने क(cid:236) बजाय समझ को (cid:255)ाथिमकता देकर, एनसीएफ परी(cid:177)ा के दबाव को कम करता है और सम(cid:250)
िवकास पर (cid:197)यान क(cid:164) ि(cid:254)त करता ह,ै जो बह(cid:242)आयामी सम(cid:250) (cid:255)गित काड(cid:170) म(cid:164) प(cid:229)रलि(cid:177)त होता है, िश(cid:177)ािवद(cid:332)
से पर े िविवध कौशल को मह(cid:194)व दते ा है। यो(cid:181)यता-आधा(cid:229)रत िश(cid:177)ा और एक लचीला, यो(cid:181)यता-आधा(cid:229)रत
पाठ्य(cid:248)म अिधगम को (cid:255)ासंिगक और (cid:211)यि(cid:265)गत बनाकर परी(cid:177)ा क(cid:236) िचंता को और कम करता है। अंत म(cid:164),
िनरंतर म(cid:208)ू यांकन सिहत मू(cid:208)यांकन सुधार एक कम तनावपूण(cid:170) वातावरण बनाते ह(cid:167) जो परी(cid:177)ा के दबाव पर
वा(cid:214)तिवक समझ को (cid:255)ाथिमकता देता है।
2. एनसीएफ के अनु(cid:322)प पाठ्यप(cid:214)ु तक(cid:164) : सम(cid:250) िवकास पर (cid:197)यान क(cid:164) ि(cid:254)त करते ह(cid:242)ए, एनसीईआरटी क(cid:236)
नई पाठ्यप(cid:214)ु तक(cid:164) स(cid:178)ं ाना(cid:194)मक, सामािजक, भावना(cid:194)मक और शारी(cid:229)रक िवकास को बढ़ावा देने वाली
गितिविधय(cid:332) को एक(cid:236)कृत करती ह(cid:167)। आकष(cid:170)क (cid:320)(cid:212)य और सलु भ भाषा िविवध िश(cid:177)ण शैिलय(cid:332) को पूरा करती

AI IN TOURISM SECTOR
1146. SHRI P. P. CHAUDHARY:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) whether smart tourism initiatives using Artificial Intelligence (AI) have been
launched by the Government to enhance visitor experience at major tourist
destinations, such as virtual guides, crowd prediction apps and multilingual
chatbots, if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the revamped Incredible India Digital Portal offers AI-powered
personalized experiences for religious and cultural tourism in Rajasthan,
particularly highlighting lesser-known destinations in Pali district, if so, the user
engagement achieved thereof;
(c) whether any partnerships have been formed with tech startups and local
innovators to develop uniquely Indian AI solutions for tourism, if so, the success
stories and innovations thereof; and
(d) whether any AI-powered pilot projects have shown promising results in
improving tourist satisfaction and local economic benefits and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
10.02.2025 945
(a) to (d): Development of Tourism is primarily undertaken by the concerned
State Governments (SGs)/Union Territory Administrations (UTA’s). However, the
Ministry of Tourism complements these efforts by providing financial assistance to
the SGs/UTA’s under its various infrastructure schemes. The Ministry is further
encouraging the SGs to include soft components involving the use of technology to
enhance visitor experience at major tourist destinations in the project proposals
formulated by them for sanctioning under such schemes.
The Ministry has launched the revamped version of Incredible India Digital
Platform (IIDP) as a comprehensive resource for travellers and stakeholders
interested in exploring the country’s rich cultural heritage, natural beauty, and
diverse attractions of the country including Rajasthan. The Incredible India Digital
Platform (IIDP) personalizes visitor experiences by offering real-time weather
updates, city exploration, and essential travel services. The portal has also
partnered with several OTAs (Online Travel Agents) and Stakeholders for seamless
booking of flights, hotels, cabs, and buses and tickets for ASI monuments.

(क) (cid:179)या सरकार ने ए(cid:204)स और आईआईटी के नेतृ(cid:194)व म(cid:164) तीन आिट(cid:170)िफिशयल इंटेिलज(cid:164)स (एआई)

10.02.2025 947
REDUCTION OF IMPORT DUTIES/TAXES ON LIFE-SAVING DRUGS
1148. SHRI BIPLAB KUMAR DEB:
SHRI DINESHBHAI MAKWANA:
DR. HEMANT VISHNU SAVARA:
SHRI LUMBA RAM:
SHRI YOGENDER CHANDOLIA:
SHRI TEJASVI SURYA:
SHRI BIDYUT BARAN MAHATO:
SHRIMATI KRITI DEVI DEBBARMAN:
SHRI DHARAMBIR SINGH:
SHRI RAVINDRA SHUKLA ALIAS RAVI KISHAN:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has taken any steps to reduce import duties or taxes
on lifesaving drugs for Rare diseases and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether there is a GST exemption in case of such imports; and
(c) the detailed process to utilize such exemptions?
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE(SHRI PANKAJ
CHOUDHARY):
(a) and (b): (i) All drugs, medicines and Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP)
used for the treatment of rare diseases listed in Statement -I are exempted from
basic customs duty and IGST, when imported by
1. individuals for personal use
10.02.2025 948
2. by Centres of Excellence listed in Statement-II, or any person or institution
recommended by any of the Centre of Excellence.
(ii) In Union Budget 2025-26, life-saving drugs or medicines at Statement-III for
rare diseases have been added to List 4 of notification No. 50/2017-Customs, which
attracts Nil Basic customs duty.
(c): The procedure prescribed under notification No. 50/2017-Customs is as
follows:
(i) For personal imports, the individual has to furnish a form (attached as
Statement-III) certified by Director General or Deputy Director General or Assistant
Director General, Health Services, New Delhi, Director of Health Services of the
State Government or the District Medical Officer/Civil Surgeon of the district before
Deputy Commissioner of Customs or the Assistant Commissioner of Customs at
the time of clearance or gives an undertaking as acceptable to the Deputy
Commissioner or the Assistant Commissioner to furnish the said certificate within
such period as may be specified by the Deputy Commissioner or the Assistant
Commissioner, failing which to pay duty leviable thereon.
(ii) For imports by any person or institution, there has to be recommendation by
any of the Centre of Excellence listed in Statement-II, certifying that the person (by
name) for whom the drugs or medicines [Drugs, Medicines or Food for Special
Medical Purposes (FSMP)] are being imported, is suffering from a rare disease (to
be specified by name) and requires these drugs or medicines [Drugs, Medicines or
Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP)] for the treatment of said rare disease.
10.02.2025 949

CREATING JOB-READY SKILLED WORKERS UNDER PMKVY 4.0
1149. SHRIMATI SHAMBHAVI:
SHRI RAJESH VERMA:
SHRI NARESH GANPAT MHASKE:
DR. SHRIKANT EKNATH SHINDE:
SHRI RAVINDRA DATTARAM WAIKAR:
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:-
(a) the details of the individuals that have been trained under Pradhan Mantri
Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) 4.0 in FY 2024-25 State-wise and the year-wise
target for skilling during the next five years;
(b) the details of the initiatives undertaken under the aforementioned scheme to
promote skill development among women, Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes
and other marginalized groups, particularly in Maharashtra and Bihar;
(c) the measures that are being implemented by the Government to enhance the
quality of training and ensure that the National Skill Qualification Framework
(NSQF) is uniformly applied across all centres;
(d) whether the Government has partnered with industries to create job-ready
skilled workers under PMKVY 4.0, if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the Government has data on the placement rate of candidates trained
under the aforementioned scheme, if so, the details thereof and the manner in
which the Government ensuring employment linkages for beneficiaries?
10.02.2025 955
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a)The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship is implementing
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 (PMKVY 4.0) since 2022-23, as a
Demand-Driven scheme to skill 1.5 crore candidates through Short-Term Training
(STT) and up-skilling and re-skilling through Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) to
youth across the country for a period of four years from 2022-23 to 2025-26.
The State-wise details of candidates trained under PMKVY 4.0 in Financial
Year (FY) 2024-25 as on 31.12.2024 are given in the enclosed Statement.
(b) The Ministry has undertaken various initiatives under PMKVY 4.0 to promote
skill development among women, Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other
marginalized groups. PMKVY 4.0 focus to improve inclusivity by ensuring that
Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), women, and other marginalized
communities can undertake skill training and eventually access gainful wage and
self-employment. Further, boarding and Lodging and transport facilities are being
provided to special groups Women and Persons with Disability (PwD) and Special
Areas, as defined in Common Norms, for training within and outside Special Areas.
In addition, Projects/ Training under Special Projects may be taken up with the
residential training targeting marginalized and vulnerable groups like women.
Conveyance facility is also permissible for Women and PwDs in case of Non-
Residential training as per Common Norms. The details of number of women,
Scheduled Castes (SCs), Scheduled Tribes (STs) and Other Backward Class
10.02.2025 956
(OBC) candidates trained under PMKVY 4.0 as on 31.12.2024 in the State of Bihar
and Maharashtra are given below:
S.N State Women SC ST OBC
o. candidate candidat candidates candidate
s trained es trained s trained
trained
1 Bihar 54,099 13,858 2,034 65,294
2 Maharasht 47,471 13,161 3,921 34,081
ra
(c) The Government of India has implemented measures under PMKVY 4.0 to
improve training quality and ensure NSQF alignment across all centres. This
includes standardized training programs, on-the-job training (OJT), and the
provision of necessary infrastructure at training centres. Trainers must have
certifications and undergo the Training of Trainers (ToT) program to maintain high
standards. Regular assessments, certification, and third-party audits further ensure
compliance and effectiveness.
(d) The scheme has collaborated with industry partners to create job-ready skilled
workers. Industry tie-ups focus on co-designed curricula and on-the-job training
(OJT). This collaboration ensures that trained candidates have exposure to real-
world industry environments, increasing their employability.
10.02.2025 957
(e) Under PMKVY scheme, placements were tracked in the Short-Term Training
(STT) component in the first three versions of the Scheme which is PMKVY 1.0,
PMKVY 2.0 and PMKVY 3.0 implemented from FY 2015-16 to FY 2021-22. Under
PMKVY 4.0, the focus is to empower our trained candidates to choose their varied
career path and they are suitably oriented for the same. However, to enable the
opportunities for employments, Skill India Digital Hub (SIDH) Portal has been
launched as a one-stop platform that integrates skilling, education, employment,
and entrepreneurship ecosystems to provide a lifelong array of services targeting
a wide range of stakeholders. Details of the trained candidates are available on
SIDH portal for connecting with potential employers. Through Skill India Digital Hub,
candidates can have access to jobs and apprenticeship opportunities. Also, On-
Job-Training (OJT) has been made an inherent component of the PMKVY.

giving me this opportunity. The Supreme Court's parameter for reservation eligibility
for BPL population is 25 per cent. वष(cid:170) 2023 म(cid:164) नीित आयोग के िहसाब स े सीमांचल िबहार का
जो ए(cid:229)रया ह,ै जहां पर सरु जापुरी मुि(cid:214)लम जाित क(cid:236) आबादी अ(cid:184)छी खासी ह ै और लगभग 65 परस(cid:164)ट
िबलो पावट(cid:234) ह,ै सरु जापुरी जाित सबस े (cid:186)यादा वंिचत ह ै । उसको पि(cid:306)म बंगाल म(cid:164) आर(cid:177)ण िमल रहा ह,ै
लेिकन िबहार म(cid:164) नह(cid:233) िमल रहा ह ै । आदरणीय (cid:255)धान म(cid:253)ं ी जी भी लगातार पसमांदा मुि(cid:214)ल(cid:204)स क(cid:236) चचा(cid:170)
करते ह(cid:167), लेिकन वहां पर न तो कॉलेज ह,ै न जॉब ह(cid:167), न इंड(cid:214)(cid:367)ी ह,ै न एएमयू के िलए फं ड िदया जा रहा
ह ै ।
Through you, I demand that the inclusion of Surjapuri community is done in
the Bihar State Reservation List, Annexure-I. I also demand for the inclusion of
Surjapuri community in the Central OBC List, and a dedicated socio-economic
survey should be done at the earliest by the NITI Aayog and the Bihar Government.
I also request that implementation of special welfare schemes targeting
education, employment, financial support for Surjapuri, as is done for similarly
placed communities in other States, should be done as early as possible.
Thank you.
DR. NAMDEO KIRSAN (GADCHIROLI-CHIMUR): Thank you, hon. Chairperson.
The pension scheme for old artists given every month by the Ministry of Culture
has been discontinued since 2020-21. These old artists, who were receiving the
pension from the Central Government, are deprived of this pension as it has been
stopped. They are humbly requesting the Government to start their pension again.
The artists were selected as beneficiaries of the scheme after going through
the application process as per the format prescribed by the Selection Committee.
10.02.2025 984
Some of those artists have got the pension. But the pension stopped subsequently.
I, therefore, humbly request the Government to restore the pension for these old
artists.
Thank you.

10.02.2025 986
SHRI SAPTAGIRI SANKAR ULAKA (KORAPUT): I rise today to bring to the
urgent attention of this House a matter of immense significance for the people of
South Odisha, namely the realignment of the sanctioned Gunupur-Theruvali
Railway Project to pass through Padmapur, Gudari and Bissam Cuttack.
We welcome this much-needed railway project in its current alignment, but it
fails to serve any meaningful purpose. Instead, it bypasses densely populated,
economically active, and historically significant areas, leaving out the very people
who would benefit from this project.
This demand has been approved by the General Manager of East Coast
Railway. The Final Location Survey has also been completed confirming that with
a minor 17 kms. deviation, the railway line can pass through Padmapur, Gudari,
Ramanaguda, Bissam Cuttack and other key blocks directly benefiting over six lakh
people in Rayagada and Gajapati districts. The current line passes through
sparsely populated and barren areas. In contrast, the realignment via Padmapur
and Bissam Cuttack would serve as a catalyst for economic growth, improving
access to trade, education, and healthcare. This region is agriculture-intensive,
commercially significant, and historically important, yet its exclusion from the
railway project is unjustifiable.
The sanctioned alignment of the Gunupur-Theruvali railway line, which
includes stations at Gunupur, Penkam, Ramanaguda, Bankili, Durgi, and Theruvali
passes through thinly inhabited and barren areas. With minor realignment, it can
pass through stations like Gunupur, Chalakamba, Padmapur, Bissam Cuttack,
Gumuda, Penkam, Ramanaguda, Bankili, Durgi and Theruvali, which follows a
10.02.2025 987
geographically logical and development-focused route directly benefiting over six
lakh people in Rayagada and Gajapati districts.
I would urge the hon. Minister of Railways to issue a formal directive to re-
examine the alignment considering that the GM of East Coast Railways had
approved the Final Location Survey. This small realignment will bring enormous
socio-economic benefits to the region and prevent yet another case of public
infrastructure failing to serve the public needs.
I would urge the Government to act immediately and not let another crucial
infrastructure project bypass the rightful aspirations of the people. Let development
be inclusive, people-centric, and equitable. Thank you so much.

SHRI V. K. SREEKANDAN (PALAKKAD): Respected Chairperson, due to non-
release of the Central share pertaining to the schemes such as the Indira Gandhi
National Old Age Pension Scheme, the Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension
Scheme, and the Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme to the State of
Kerala, thousands of people in the State are not getting the Central share of these
schemes for many months, running into over 15 months.
Therefore, it is urged that the Government may release the Central share of
10.02.2025 996
these schemes to the beneficiaries urgently. Thank you, Madam.

MATTERS UNDER RULE 377
माननीय सभापित : िजन माननीय सद(cid:214)य(cid:332) को आज िनयम 377 के अधीन मामल(cid:332) को उठान े क(cid:236)
अनमु ित (cid:255)दान क(cid:236) गई ह,ै वे अपने मामल(cid:332) के अनमु ोिदत पाठ को तुर(cid:198)त (cid:211)यि(cid:265)गत (cid:322)प स े सभा पटल पर
रखने क(cid:236) कृपा कर (cid:164) ।
(i) Need to establish universities and technological colleges in Deoria
Parliamentary Constituency, Uttar Pradesh
SHRI SHASHANK MANI (DEORIA): I wish to highlight the lack of higher
educational institutions in Deoria Parliamentary Constituency, Uttar Pradesh. Many
students are compelled to migrate to other regions for quality education, causing
financial strain and brain drain. I request the Ministry of Education to establish
universities and technical colleges in Deoria to provide accessible and quality
education to our youth.
(ii) Need to take comprehensive measures to achieve a tuberculosis free
India by 2025
SHRI P. P. CHAUDHARY (PALI): Under visionary leadership of Prime Minister,
India has made significant progress in tuberculosis control. I would like to draw the
 Treated as laid on the Table.
10.02.2025 1006
attention of the Hon'ble Minister of Health and Family Welfare to further strengthen
our fight against TB. WHO's Global TB Report 2024 shows India's remarkable
achievement with a 17.7% decline in TB incidence from 2015 to 2023, surpassing
the global decline of 8.3%. TB-related deaths decreased by 21.4% during this
period. Through initiatives like National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme, the
100-Day TB Elimination Campaign, and Nikshay Poshan Yojana, which has
disbursed Rs. 2,781 crores to support 1 crore beneficiaries, we have made
substantial progress. However, to achieve a TB-free India by 2025, I urge the
Ministry to increase program allocations, expand AI-enabled diagnostic facilities
beyond the existing 7,767 testing centers, strengthen the supply chain of quality-
assured TB drugs, and address co-existing conditions like malnutrition and HIV
through a comprehensive approach. Special focus should be given to
decentralizing TB services and supporting frontline workers to ensure treatment
reaches our rural populations.
(iii) Need to increase the assistance amount under National Social
Assistance Programme Scheme
SHRI MUKESHKUMAR CHANDRAKAANT DALAL (SURAT): The National
Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) is a centrally sponsored scheme that caters
to the needs of 3.09 crore BPL beneficiaries. Currently, under this scheme, eligible
elderly individuals, widows, and persons with disabilities living below the poverty
line receive a monthly pension ranging from ₹200 to ₹500. Since the inception of
this programme, its process and system have undergone several changes.
10.02.2025 1007
However, the last increase in the assistance amount was made in 2012. Apart from
central assistance, states and union territories provide additional financial aid
ranging from ₹50 to ₹3,200 per beneficiary per month under the NSAP pension
schemes. In many states, beneficiaries receive a monthly pension of ₹1,000, but
this amount remains inadequate for sustenance, raising serious concerns. Dr. Mihir
Shah Committee (2012), Sumit Bose Committee (2016), NITI Aayog (2020), and
the Rural Development Department’s evaluation study (2021) have also
recommended an increase in pension amounts. I urge the Government to increase
the assistance amount under all NSAP schemes and raise the pension to ₹2,000
per month, ensuring that the poor and elderly sections of society receive adequate
financial support for a dignified life.

district, Madhya Pradesh

10.02.2025 1010
(viii) Need to take comprehensive measures to ensure resolving the issues
with regard to Darjeeling Hills, Terai and Dooars regions in West Bengal
SHRI RAJU BISTA (DARJEELING): The Darjeeling hills, Terai, and Dooars have
unique history, geography, and culture, distinct from the rest of West Bengal. The
successive West Bengal Governments have not taken appropriate welfare
measures in favor of these people of these regions. This has led to repeated
demands for political autonomy, including four major agitations for Gorkhaland. In
1988, the Darjeeling Gorkha Hill Council (DGHC) was formed. In 2011, the
Gorkhaland Territorial Administration (GTA) was established. Both the DGHC and
GTA were undermined by the West Bengal Government, and not allowed to
function autonomously. When people demand better governance and their
constitutional rights, they are labelled as foreigners. Today, while Rohingya and
illegal immigrants from Bangladesh are being settled in the region, the indigenous
population is at risk of becoming a minority in their own land. This is a sensitive
"Chicken Neck" area and the changing demography is a threat to the national
security of India. The people of the region too are Indian citizens who have
contributed immensely to nation-building. I request the Central Government to
intervene and expedite a permanent political solution for these regions. So that the
indigenous Gorkha, Adivasi, Rajbangshi, Bengali, Koche, Meche, Rabha, Toto can
have a safe and secure future.
10.02.2025 1011

10.02.2025 1012
(xi) Need to take steps to appoint chairman of Mahanadi Water Dispute
Tribunal (MWDT) at the earliest
SHRI BHARTRUHARI MAHTAB (CUTTACK): River Mahanadi an interstate river
which flows from Chhattisgarh eastward is the life of Odisha. Today Odisha is
threatened due to gradual decrease in flow from upstream, mainly from the
catchment of Chhattisgarh. Inflow into Hirakud Reservoir has been gradually
reducing in the non-monsoon period. This reduction of flow has been intimated to
Ministry of Jal Shakti. As both the States Odisha and Chhattisgarh could not reach
an agreement to sharing of water amicably, on the direction of Supreme Court of
India in 2018, Mahanadi Water Dispute Tribunal was formed by the Union
Government and complaint of Odisha was referred to it. The tenure of MWDT was
for 3 years and was extended to for two more years upto 11.03.2023 and
subsequently has been extended upto 13th April 2026 or till the submission of report
whichever is earlier. The 43rd sitting of the Tribunal was held on 22nd Feb 2024.
Justice A.M. Khanwilkar, Chairman, Mahanadi Water Dispute Tribunal has
resigned from the post since March 2024 and that post is lying vacant. In such an
eventuality, the Tribunal is in limbo and Odisha while waiting for a decision is
suffering for want of water. I urge upon the Government to take immediate steps to
appoint a Chairman of MWDT so that the decision be given at the earliest.
(xii) Need to impress upon the Government of Kerala to review the
decision to set up Brewery Project at Elappally in Palakkad, Kerala
SHRI V. K. SREEKANDAN (PALAKKAD): The recent decision of Government of
10.02.2025 1013
Kerala to allow to set up a brewery over an area of 26 acres which includes 8 acres
of agricultural land in Elappully Panchayat, Palakkad district will seriously affect
farming and water availability there. The people of this area solely depend upon
groundwater for drinking and for their all other needs as it is a water scarcity area.
One of the conditions agreed that State government shall provide millions of litres
of water from Malampuzha dam for the unit whereas water availability in
Malampuzha dam is insufficient to meet even drinking and farming purposes. There
is fear that effluents generated by proposed brewery unit will be dumped into the
Korayar River. The people of Elappully Panchayat had resisted the attempt to setup
a brewery in Elappully in the year 2020 and the proposal had to be withdrawn.
Moreover, the proposal to set up a brewery was cleared by the State government
without undertaking any environmental impact study. On three accounts, such as
water deficit, loss of farming, and impact on environment, this proposal should not
be allowed. Therefore, it is urged that State government may be asked to stop this
project in public interest.

(xv) Need to establish PM Vishwakarma Skill Centre in Mannar, Kerala to
revive the traditional brass industry of the region
SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH (MAVELIKKARA): I wish to bring the attention of
the Central Government towards the urgent need to revitalize the traditional brass
industry in Mannar, Chengannur, Kerala. Renowned as the "Bell Metal Town,"
Mannar has a long-standing heritage in brass craftsmanship, sustaining thousands
of artisans. However, this industry is in decline due to rising production costs,
shortage of skilled labor, competition from machine-made products, and
inadequate market support. To revive this sector, I urge the Central Government to
introduce a special package providing financial assistance, raw material subsidies,
and modern infrastructure. Establishing a PM Vishwakarma Skill Center in the
region will be instrumental in training artisans, preserving traditional skills, and
incorporating modern techniques. Additionally, I request the introduction of Region-
Specific Product Certification for Mannar brassware to enhance its market value
and secure Geographical Indication (GI) status, protecting its authenticity. A
targeted Incentive Scheme with tax rebates, low-interest loans, and export
promotion initiatives will further aid artisans and small-scale manufacturers. I
appeal to the Central Government to take urgent steps to safeguard and promote
this iconic industry, ensuring its sustainability and the economic welfare of its
artisans.

(xix) Need to recognize ‘Saridharam’ and ‘Sarna Dharam’ as separate
religions followed by Santhals and other Tribes
SHRI KALIPADA SAREN KHERWAL (JHARGRAM): As we know the Santhals
are the largest homogeneous tribe of India, and they mostly inhabit in the States of
West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha, Bihar, Assam and Tripura. The Santhals and
other Adivasi communities profess and practice their own religion "SARIDHARAM"
and “SARNA DHARAM" since time immemorial. Although the right to practice,
10.02.2025 1018
profess and propagate our own religion is our Fundamental Right as per the Article
25 and 26 of the Constitution of India; yet the SANTHALS and other Adivasi
communities have not been able to find recognition for their own religion
“SARIDHARAM" and “SARNA DHARAM” in India. It is noteworthy to mention here
that the West Bengal Assembly has already introduced and approved a Bill as per
'Motion Under Parliamentary Rule 185' for recognition of “SARIDHARAM” for
Santhals and “SARNA DHARAM” for other Adivasis on 17th February 2023.
Therefore, to honour the religious sentiments of the Santhals and other Adivasis. I,
strongly demand to the Government of India for recognition of “SARIDHARAM” for
Santhals and “SARNA DHARAM” for other Adivasis and also demand for allotment
of separate religion Column Code for “SARIDHARAM” and “SARNA DHARAM” in
the Data Collection Form to be used in the forthcoming Census of India.
(xx) Need for doubling of railway line between Villupuram and Katpadi
Junction in Tamil Nadu
SHRI C. N. ANNADURAI (TIRUVANNAMALAI): I wish to draw attention to the fact
that between Villupuram to Katpadi Junction there is single railway line which
causes lot of problems to the daily commuters. Passengers get delayed to reach
their destination because incoming and outgoing trains travel on the same line. This
is the situation when we claim modern means of transport and communication.
Therefore, it is needless to emphasise over the need for doubling the railway line
between Villupuram to Katpadi Junction. The project so suggested would not only
the benefit to the daily commuters but open the railway transportation to various
10.02.2025 1019
junctions viz., Salem, Coimbatore, Bangalore, Ernaculam, Trichy, Madurai, etc,
thereby linking railway connectivity to so many routes. I, therefore, request the
Hon’ble Minister of Railways to have a positive look on the above mentioned project
as soon as possible.
(xxi) Need to establish an Ayurvedic Institute under National AYUSH
Mission in Nandyal, Andhra Pradesh
DR. BYREDDY SHABARI (NANDYAL): The Government of India’s National
AYUSH Mission (NAM) has made commendable strides in promoting Ayurvedic
education and healthcare. However, significant regional disparities persist. States
like Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Gujarat have a strong presence of Ayurvedic
institutes, while Andhra Pradesh lags with just there is an insufficient number to
cater to the state's growing population. In the past five years, no new Ayurvedic
institutes have been established in Andhra Pradesh, highlighting the lack of focus
on accessible healthcare for underserved areas. Districts like Nandyal, with limited
healthcare infrastructure, are in dire need of such institutions to provide both
traditional treatments and education for future practitioners. NAM provides central
assistance to states with inadequate facilities, but Andhra Pradesh has not fully
benefitted from this support. Therefore, I urge the Ministry of AYUSH to prioritize
the establishment of Ayurvedic institutes in underserved regions like Nandyal. A
collaborative effort with the State Government could help bridge this gap, ensuring
equitable access to affordable, holistic healthcare and revitalizing Ayurveda in the
region.

(xxiv) Need to constitute an independent judicial Commission to investigate
into irregularities linked to the National Assessment and Accreditation
Council (NAAC)
SHRI RAJA RAM SINGH (KARAKAT): The recent arrest of JNU professor Rajeev
Sijariya and others in a bribery case linked to the National Assessment and
Accreditation Council (NAAC) has exposed deep-rooted corruption in higher
education accreditation. This incident, involving alleged bribes for inflated NAAC
ratings, undermines the credibility of India's higher education system and
compromises academic quality. Such practices not just erode trust in institutions
but also devalue the degrees awarded to students. Under the leadership of UGC
Chairman Mamidala Jagadesh Kumar, concerns have been raised about the
commercialization of higher education, with allegations of academic standards
being compromised for financial gains. This particular incident not only highlights
the urgent need for transparency but also immediate intervention of Union
Government. Thereby, taking appropriate measures against UGC Chairman to
restore integrity in India's education system. The accreditation-based policy
prescribed by New Education Policy has acted like a catalyst for this massive
corruption as it incentivises institutions to fiddle with rules to get accreditation at
any cost. I demand an independent judicial commission to look into the acts of
10.02.2025 1022
omission and commission by NAAC. The role UGC Chairperson Mamidala
Jagadish Kumar too needs to be investigated as he is the architect of destruction
of higher education in India.

income tax exemption threshold of Rs.12 lakh is an attempt to placate a small
portion of Indian middle-class who are living in cities. Their opportunities are less
and salaries are low. They may enjoy for a year or two. They will be happy about
the extra thousands in their pockets. But the alarming rate of inflation, especially
on food and essentials will catch up in two years if growth stagnates. This measure
is a mere short-term economic sugar rush and not a sustainable solution.
Sir, India's per capita income in 2020-23 was Rs.1.7 lakh per year or you can
say Rs.14,000 per month. In rural India, it was even lower. It was Rs.85,000 per
year, which was just Rs.7,000 per month. So, for the remaining 98.25 per cent of
Indians, this Rs.12 lakh relief in income tax is a non-issue. They are the real Indians
who have been ignored by this Budget. They are unemployed, they have no jobs
or they earn just enough to survive. They also suffer from rising food and essential
costs, just like these people who are getting Rs.12 lakh benefit on income tax. While
I welcome the reduction in income tax, the relief is to a small portion of the middle-
class. It is very easy to get confused, and one can assume that only those paying
income tax are bearing the brunt of nation-building. I would assert that every single
citizen of this country is paying dearly through indirect taxes. Reality check shows
that with your projected growth, we will not have a growth rate of eight per cent
every year for at least next 10 years. We will not be able to see income growing
 Expunged as ordered by the Chair.
10.02.2025 1027
fast enough to match the inflation.
Sir, we also call the taxation idea superficial because the Budget fails to
address the growing wealth divide. The top one per cent of India's population holds
more than 40 per cent of national wealth. Instead of a progressive taxation of the
ultra-rich, this Budget, like always, relies on high indirect taxes on essential goods,
forcing the common man to bleed dry for the mere survival. Top five per cent of the
Indian population holds nearly 60 per cent of all wealth. If we calculate the per
capita GDP of the remaining 95 per cent against the 40 per cent of the wealth, the
reality of our economy is that we are lagging even behind the sub-Saharan African
countries.
Look at the incredible high fuel prices. Throughout the COVID-19 time, the
Government made bumper profits of taxes excised on fuel when the global markets
were low. The total contribution of the petroleum sector to your exchequer in 2019-
20 was Rs.5,55,370 crore. In 2020-21, it was Rs. 6,72,780 crore. In 2021-22, it was
Rs. 7,74,425 crore. In 2022-23, it was Rs. 7,48,718 crore. In 2023-24, it was Rs.
7,51,155.9 crore. In the last six months alone, you have collected nearly Rs.
3,39,185.5 crore, which is a provisional amount. Since you have made huge profits
in the price of petrol, especially with the Ukraine-Russia war going on, what did you
do? First time in our country, we heard the terminology ‘windfall taxes’. When
Russia was selling fuel at $60 against a market price of $120, you allowed Reliance
Industries to make windfall profits. You allowed all your friends to make windfall
profits. You could have passed the 50 per cent price reduction to our poor Indians.
With the increasing inflation now, you have so much of money in your kitty in the
10.02.2025 1028
name of excise and cess. Why do you not remove it and give it to the common
man? Sir, 98 per cent of the population will benefit out of this. But, what have you
done? You have done welfare cuts and neglect of work.
Now, again, our Finance Minister, Shrimati Nirmala Sitharaman, is boasting
that she has reduced the fiscal deficit. It is 4.5 per cent of the GDP. At what cost
have you reached this figure of curtailing the fiscal deficit? You have cut the
MGNREGS funding, which remains stagnant at Rs. 86,000 crore, despite a record
low of unemployment. The SC/ST education funds are unchanged, making higher
education for them unaffordable. The food prices are increasing. The poor man is
suffering. But, what have you done? The food subsidy has been cut from Rs.
2,05,250 crore to Rs. 2,03,000 crore. The budget for Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana
has been slashed by 35 per cent, affecting affordable housing. You claim in the
President’s speech that you are going to build 10 lakh houses. I wonder, with this
fiscal cut, how you are going to manage to do that! Jal Jeevan Mission funding is
down to Rs. 67,000 crore, lower than spending in the last two years.
Hon. Chairperson, Sir, in 2019, they created a new Ministry called Jal Sakthi
Ministry. Everyone wondered! What are you doing? Why are you renaming it? But,
you said, ‘we are renaming it because we are going to interlink the rivers of India
and ensure there is no drought, there is no flooding in any part of the country’.
Please give us a list. It has been 11 years now. How many rivers have you
interlinked? How many droughts have you stopped? How many floodings have you
stopped? Nothing, zilch. At a time when spending on mental health programmes
should be increased because of inflation and joblessness, you have reduced it from
10.02.2025 1029
Rs. 133 crore to Rs. 90 crore, ignoring the growing crisis. Employment generation
is the need of the hour, but no major initiatives have been taken for job creation,
despite 2.5 crore people being unemployed. The MSMEs, which provide 45 per
cent of India’s export and employ 7.5 crore people, receive no tax relief or any
support from this Budget. Sixty to seventy lakh young people enter the job market
annually. Yet, no plans exist to address the employment needs. All the PSUs have
been sold probably, or are in the process of being sold. Earlier, we used the public
sector companies to generate employment for our youth. But now, even that is not
in your hands. Now, we have to go and beg Adanis, Ambanis, and Tatas to provide
employment for our youth.
Sir, railway is very, very important for us. What have you done? You have
reduced the funding for railway projects from Rs. 34,000 crore to Rs. 32,000 crore.
We really need more investment, especially when we have seen more than 200 rail
accidents in the last five years and when the need has come to improve the Kavach
system.

To top it all, what have you done? The Finance Minister has done double

in India that are producing the iPhones. Right now, all we are doing is assembling

Another sad thing is that even those assembly lines are imported from China.
Any new electronic unit is now coming from China and we are just assembling it.
We do not even have the know-how of how to make a power adapter in India? It
has to be imported from China. That is the sad state. … (Interruptions)
The trade deficit with China is still high. For the Financial Year 2024, India’s
State deficit with China surged to $85.1 billion, and we continue importing critical
components for electronics, pharmaceuticals and our machinery. In fact, for every
mobile phone, its display, the battery, the processor are still being imported from
China. We need to make sure that we have the facilities. The automobile sector is
mostly dependent on imports. We have to become self-reliant. When are we going
to become self-reliant? This story reminds me of what happened when the United
States imposed tariffs on China for certain goods. China did nothing. China went
straight to Brazil and assembled there because Brazil had a treaty with the US for
free export. This is the same thing that we are following. We are assembling in India
all the Chinese parts.
Even the supply chains are dominated by three players, including Ambanis
and Adanis. There is no one else. If any multinational has to step into India, they

बड़ी छूट दी गई थी । आयकर क(cid:236) दर घटाकर 22 (cid:255)ितशत और कई कं पनीज़ के िलए 15 (cid:255)ितशत कर
